item.title: 😤 当你最喜欢的项目也进入墓地时的那种感觉...... | 😤 That feeling when your favorite project too goes in the graveyard...
item.description:
一旦暂停，jmfayard 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，jmfayard 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jmfayard 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jmfayard 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 jmfayard：

降低 jmfayard 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 jmfayard 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, jmfayard will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, jmfayard will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by jmfayard will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If jmfayard is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag jmfayard:

Make all posts by jmfayard less visible

jmfayard consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging jmfayard will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: jmfayard

item.title: 安全云迁移的 6 个关键要素 | 6 Key Elements For A Secure Cloud Migration
item.description:
如果您从一开始就投入资源，安全的云迁移是可能的。 云迁移中安全第一的方法假定您迁移、重构和重新架构的所有内容都会产生一个高度安全的环境，从而最大限度地降低违规、运营损失和数据泄漏的风险。

在公共云中，安全性和合规性是共同的责任。 这就是为什么必须确定您需要注意的元素以确保在云中正确保护您的业务数据的原因。

继续阅读以了解有关构建安全至上的云战略以及应成为其基石的六个要素的更多信息。

这是我们关于云迁移的系列文章中的第二篇 -查看第一篇以了解有关网络的更多信息。

什么是安全的云迁移？

云环境可以快速变化，动态地进出合规性。

安全第一的云计算方法侧重于对威胁的持续监控和管理，以确保组织始终掌握所有潜在风险。 此外，它还涉及通过自动化策略、流程和控制来了解这些危险并采取行动。

实现持续的安全第一合规状态需要现代工具、技术和流程的组合。 以下是您的云战略应包括的六个关键要素，以促进和改善您的安全状况。

安全云迁移的 6 个组成部分

所有云服务提供商 (CSP) 共享存储在云中的资源的安全性和合规性所需的一组最低限度的最佳实践。

云安全态势管理(CSPM) 解决方案有助于将云资源配置与此类最佳实践进行比较。 此外，他们还可以发现由于临时更改或恶意意图而导致的任何配置偏差。

如果您希望保持云中立，您可以选择第三方 CSPM 工具，例如 Palo Alto、ZScaler、Orca Security、Wiz Security 或 Secberus。

因此，它们有助于确保所有组件的高级别安全评分。

因此，理想情况下，您的云环境应该包括一个 CSPM 工具，并努力达到规定的最低安全性和合规性分数。 根据经验，NIST 网络安全框架的第 3 级（“已定义”）或更高级别应该是一个不错的选择。

端点检测和响应

端点检测和响应(EDR) 是一种网络安全技术，可以持续监控最终用户设备以检测和响应勒索软件和恶意软件等威胁。

云部署中的每个虚拟机都应该有一个正确安装和配置的 Endpoint Security 解决方案。

如果您选择与供应商无关的方法，您仍然可以获得范围广泛的 EDR 产品，例如 Crowd Strike、Sentinel One、Trend Micro、Broadcom 等。

为所有部署的虚拟机安装 EDR 工具应该是通过 CSPM 工具和策略即代码实施的策略的一部分。

密钥管理和秘密管理

您的云迁移计划应包括保护敏感或秘密配置。

理想情况下，您应该使用云提供的秘密管理服务将此类数据存储在中央位置。 此类工具允许公司安全地存储、传输和管理数据，例如密码、加密密钥、SSH 密钥、API 密钥、数据库凭据、令牌和证书。

密钥管理和适当的 FIPS 140.2 硬件安全模块应支持此设置。

所有云提供商都提供功能相同的机密和密钥管理服务； 您还可以从众多第三方工具中进行选择。

策略即代码

策略即代码的想法涉及用高级语言编写代码来管理和自动化策略。 这种形式可以更轻松地使用软件开发的最佳实践，例如版本控制和自动化测试和部署。

您的策略代码可能包含的规则示例如下：

  • 避免在虚拟机上使用公共 IP。
  • 所有存储必须使用存储在您的密钥管理解决方案 (KMS) 中的提供的加密密钥
  • 不允许使用公共存储对象。
  • 必须在所有API 和Web 应用程序前面使用Web 应用程序防火墙。

在整理最佳实践列表时，您可以参考行业标准，例如 CIS 基准或 CISA/NSA Kubernetes 强化指南。 然后，您可以通过 IaC 部署此类策略并测试它们的有效性。

所有云资源都会生成日志记录数据。 安全信息和事件管理 (SIEM) 技术从多个来源收集事件日志数据，帮助您识别异常活动并快速采取适当的措施。

您的云部署应该集成一个端到端的 SIEM 解决方案，并使用它来分析所有组件的日志数据。 通过通知提醒，您将能够发现可疑活动并当场解决它们。

此类别中的一些推荐解决方案包括 IBM Security QRadar、Datadog Security Monitoring、AT&T Cyber​​security、FortiSIEM 等。

安全保证和合规性

所有主要的云服务提供商都可以确保符合 SOC2 Type II、ISO127001、PCI DSS（数据存储解决方案）等流行标准。 此认证可以作为您的安全保证和合规性要求的基准。

在构建您在该领域的工作时，请关注两个关键的可交付成果：

  1. 确保您获得所有基础架构元素的 CIS 基准分数，并且没有违反“中等”以上的最佳实践。 目标是至少 90% 的已实施检查具有通过所选 CSPM 解决方案衡量的及格分数。
  2. 根据 NIST 1.1 网络安全框架检查您的环境，再次瞄准最低成熟度级别 3（“定义”），理想情况下扩展到级别 4（“管理”）或 5（“优化”）。

如果你使用 Kubernetes，一个有用的工具可能是CAST AI 的容器安全报告。 通过自动扫描您的集群配置，它会检查您是否符合基本行业标准和最佳实践。

检查合规性后，您可以为渗透测试制定时间表。 此网络安全练习可帮助您找到黑客可能利用的系统防御中的任何薄弱环节。 渗透测试应该是内部和外部的——理想情况下，由于问题的优先级排序，可以指导您的后续步骤。

启动安全的云迁移

创建安全第一的云战略可以最大限度地降低网络攻击的可能性，保护您的宝贵资产，并提高您的整体业务敏捷性。

您可以通过结合概述的解决方案和技术类型为您的云部署构建可靠的安全结构——投资肯定会得到回报。



原文:

Secure cloud migration is possible if you dedicate resources to it from the start. A security-first approach in cloud migration assumes that everything you migrate, refactor, and re-architect produces a highly secure environment, minimizing the risks of a breach, loss of operations, and data leaks. 

In the public cloud, security and compliance are shared responsibilities. That’s why it’s essential to identify the elements you need to take care of to ensure proper protection of your business data in the cloud. 

Read on to learn more about building a security-first cloud strategy and the six elements that should be its cornerstones. 

This is the second article in our series about cloud migration - check out the first one to learn more about networking.

What is a secure cloud migration?

Cloud environments can change rapidly, moving in and out of compliance dynamically. 

A security-first approach to cloud computing focuses on ongoing monitoring and management of threats to ensure the organization stays on top of all potential risks. In addition, it involves understanding and acting on these dangers through automated policies, processes, and controls.

Achieving the state of continuous security-first compliance calls for a combination of modern tools, techniques, and processes. Here are the six key elements your cloud strategy should include to promote and improve your security posture. 

6 components of a secure cloud migration

1. Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM)

All cloud service providers (CSPs) share a minimum set of best practices required for the security and compliance of resources stored in the cloud. 

Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) solutions help to compare cloud resource configurations against such best practices. In addition, they can spot any configuration drifts due to ad-hoc changes or malicious intent. 

Microsoft Azure comes with a CSPM solution for its cloud resources, but also AWS and GCP have parts of related functionalities in place. If you prefer to remain cloud-neutral, you can choose a third-party CSPM tool, such as Palo Alto, ZScaler, Orca Security, Wiz Security, or Secberus.

CSPM solutions can read and check your cloud configurations, notify you of the issues in need of remediation, and provide detailed findings and recommendations. As a result, they help to ensure a high-level security score across all components.

So ideally, your cloud environments should include a CSPM tool and strive to achieve a minimum prescribed security and compliance score. As a rule of thumb, Level 3 (“Defined”) or higher of the NIST Cybersecurity Framework should be a good choice. 

Endpoint Detection and Response 

Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) is a cybersecurity technology that continuously monitors end-user devices to detect and respond to threats like ransomware and malware.

Each Virtual Machine in your cloud deployment should have a properly installed and configured Endpoint Security solution. 

Microsoft Azure provides Defender for Windows, but there is also a version for Linux. If you choose a vendor-agnostic approach, you still get a wide range of EDR products such as Crowd Strike, Sentinel One, Trend Micro, Broadcom, and many others.

Having an EDR tool installed for all deployed VMs should be a part of the strategy enforced through both CSPM tools and Policy as Code. 

Key management and secrets management

Your cloud migration plan should include securing sensitive or secret configurations. 

Ideally, you should store such data in a central location using a cloud-provided secret management service. Such tools allow companies to securely store, transmit, and manage data like passwords, encryption keys, SSH keys, API keys, database credentials, tokens, and certificates. 

Key management and an appropriate FIPS 140.2 Hardware Security Module should support this setup. 

All cloud providers have functionally equivalent services for secrets and key management; you can also choose from numerous third-party tools.

Policy-as-code 

The idea of policy-as-code involves writing code in a high-level language to manage and automate policies. This form makes it easier to use best practices for software development, like version control and automated testing and deployment.

Examples of the rules your policy as-code could include are as follows:

  • Avoid using public IPs on Virtual Machines. 
  • All storage must use provided encryption keys stored in your key management solution (KMS)
  • Public storage objects are not allowed.
  • Web application firewall must be used in front of all API and web applications.

When collating your list of best practices, you can draw on industry standards such as CIS Benchmarks or CISA/NSA Kubernetes Hardening Guide. Then, you can deploy such policies through IaC and test them for efficacy. 

All cloud resources generate logging data. Security information and event management (SIEM) technology collects event log data from multiple sources, helping you identify unusual activity and take appropriate action quickly. 

SIEM systems have different functions, but in general, they reduce cyber risks by keeping track of how users act, limiting access attempts, and making compliance reports.

Your cloud deployment should integrate an end-to-end SIEM solution and use it to analyze log data for all components. Alerted by notifications, you’ll be able to discover suspicious activities and address them on the spot. 

Some of the recommended solutions in this category include IBM Security QRadar, Datadog Security Monitoring, AT&T Cybersecurity, FortiSIEM, and more. 

Security assurance and compliance

All key cloud service providers can ensure compliance with popular standards such as SOC2 Type II, ISO127001, PCI DSS (Data Storage Solution), and many others. This certification can be a baseline for your Security Assurance and Compliance requirements.

When structuring your work in this area, focus on the two key deliverables:

  1. Ensure you get a CIS Benchmark score for all infrastructure elements, with no best practice violations above “medium”. Aim for a minimum of 90% implemented checks with a passing score as measured by your selected CSPM solution. 
  2. Check your environment against the NIST 1.1 Cybersecure Framework, again aiming for a minimum maturity level of 3 (“Defined”), ideally expanding to Level 4 (“Managed”) or 5 (“Optimized”). 

If you use Kubernetes, a useful tool here could be CAST AI’s container security report. By automatically scanning your cluster configurations, it checks your compliance with essential industry standards and best practices. 

Once you check your compliance, you can establish a timeline for penetration testing. This cybersecurity exercise helps you find any weak spots in your system's defenses that hackers could use. Penetration testing should be internal and external—and ideally, guide your next steps thanks to issue prioritization. 

Launch a secure cloud migration

Creating a security-first cloud strategy minimizes the odds of cyber attacks, protects your valuable assets, and improves your overall business agility. 

You can build a solid security structure for your cloud deployment by combining the outlined types of solutions and techniques – the investment will certainly pay off.
item.author: castai

item.title: 创建一个使用 ChatGPT 创建其他应用程序的应用程序🤯 | Create an Application That Creates Other Applications with ChatGPT🤯
item.description:
您是否厌倦了花费大量时间从头开始编写新应用程序？ 如果我告诉你你可以自己创建新的应用程序怎么办？

这听起来好得令人难以置信，但借助 ChatGPT 的强大功能，您完全可以做到这一点。

在本文中，我们将深入研究基于 AI 的应用程序开发领域，并向您展示如何创建一个应用程序，该应用程序可以使用 ChatGPT 创建其他应用程序。

准备好？ 我们走吧！

创建其他应用程序的应用程序

最后我会留下 GitHub 存储库的链接，这样你也可以使用这个应用程序。

我们创建了一个应用程序，它使用 ChatGPT API 根据我们的需要创建应用程序。

 using OpenAI_API; using Test; OpenAIAPI api = new OpenAIAPI("YOUR_API_KEY"); // shorthand var source = ""; await api.Completions.StreamCompletionAsync( new CompletionRequest("REQUEST_APPLICATION", Model.DavinciText, 200, 0.5, presencePenalty: 0.1, frequencyPenalty: 0.1), res => source += res.ToString()); //Console.WriteLine(source); var compiler = new Compiler(); var runner = new Runner(); byte[] compiled = compiler.Compile(source); string[] none = Array.Empty<string>(); runner.Execute(compiled, none); Console.ReadLine();

例如，我们可以尝试：

创建一个 C# 代码，要求我输入一个数字并检查它是偶数还是奇数。 如果是偶数，则必须返回“它是偶数”。 如果它是奇数，它必须通过控制台返回“it is odd”。

 using OpenAI_API; using Test; OpenAIAPI api = new OpenAIAPI("YOUR_API_KEY"); // shorthand var source = ""; await api.Completions.StreamCompletionAsync( new CompletionRequest("Create a C# code that asks me for a number with red text and check if it is odd or even. If it is even, it will have to return \"it is even\" in yellow. If it is odd, it will have to return by console \"is odd\" in blue. After answering me, you must ask me for another number in red and tell me if it is even or odd. Infinitely.", Model.DavinciText, 200, 0.5, presencePenalty: 0.1, frequencyPenalty: 0.1), res => source += res.ToString()); //Console.WriteLine(source); var compiler = new Compiler(); var runner = new Runner(); byte[] compiled = compiler.Compile(source); string[] none = Array.Empty<string>(); runner.Execute(compiled, none); Console.ReadLine();

如果我们运行并尝试：

它运作良好！ 让我们尝试一些不同的东西：

创建一个 C# 应用程序，一个一个地要求我输入 3 个数字。 然后返回这三个数字的平均值。

 using OpenAI_API; using Test; OpenAIAPI api = new OpenAIAPI("YOUR_API_KEY"); // shorthand var source = ""; await api.Completions.StreamCompletionAsync( new CompletionRequest("Create a C# application that asks me for 3 numbers one by one. Then return the average of those three numbers.", Model.DavinciText, 2000, 0.5, presencePenalty: 0.1, frequencyPenalty: 0.1), res => source += res.ToString()); //Console.WriteLine(source); var compiler = new Compiler(); var runner = new Runner(); byte[] compiled = compiler.Compile(source); string[] none = Array.Empty<string>(); runner.Execute(compiled, none); Console.ReadLine();

让我们看看结果：

让我们尝试一些更困难的事情。

创建一个 C# 应用程序，它要求我提供指示 csv 文件的路径。 第一列是用户的名称。 第二列是他们完成的登录次数。 输入 csv 文件的路径后，应用程序应输出一个表，其中包含登录次数最多的 5 个用户的名称和登录次数。

对于这个例子，我们准备了一个简单的示例表，其中包含 100 个名称和随机数，然后我们将其导出为 csv：

并做了！

这些只是一些简单的想法，但它们可以像您希望的那样复杂。 正如我在开头所说，在这里您可以访问 GitHub 上的存储库。 如果您设法创造了一些有趣的东西，请将其留在评论部分！

一旦暂停，bytehide 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，bytehide 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，bytehide 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 bytehide 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 bytehide 的方法：

降低 bytehide 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 bytehide 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Are you tired of spending many hours coding new applications from scratch? What if I told you that you can create new apps on your own?

It may sound too good to be true, but with the power of ChatGPT, you can do exactly that.

In this article, we’ll delve into the world of AI-based app development and show you how to create an app that creates other apps with ChatGPT.

Ready? Let’s go!

The App that creates other Apps

At the end I will leave the link to the GitHub repository so you can also use this application.

We have created an application that creates applications based on what we want using the ChatGPT API.

using OpenAI_API;
using Test; OpenAIAPI api = new OpenAIAPI("YOUR_API_KEY"); // shorthand var source = ""; await api.Completions.StreamCompletionAsync( new CompletionRequest("REQUEST_APPLICATION", Model.DavinciText, 200, 0.5, presencePenalty: 0.1, frequencyPenalty: 0.1), res => source += res.ToString()); //Console.WriteLine(source); var compiler = new Compiler();
var runner = new Runner(); byte[] compiled = compiler.Compile(source);
string[] none = Array.Empty<string>();
runner.Execute(compiled, none); Console.ReadLine();

And for example we can try:

Create a C# code that asks me for a number and check if it is even or odd. If it is even, it will have to return “it is even”. If it is odd, it will have to return by console “it is odd”.

using OpenAI_API;
using Test; OpenAIAPI api = new OpenAIAPI("YOUR_API_KEY"); // shorthand var source = ""; await api.Completions.StreamCompletionAsync( new CompletionRequest("Create a C# code that asks me for a number with red text and check if it is odd or even. If it is even, it will have to return \"it is even\" in yellow. If it is odd, it will have to return by console \"is odd\" in blue. After answering me, you must ask me for another number in red and tell me if it is even or odd. Infinitely.", Model.DavinciText, 200, 0.5, presencePenalty: 0.1, frequencyPenalty: 0.1), res => source += res.ToString()); //Console.WriteLine(source); var compiler = new Compiler();
var runner = new Runner(); byte[] compiled = compiler.Compile(source);
string[] none = Array.Empty<string>();
runner.Execute(compiled, none); Console.ReadLine();

If we run and try it:

It works well! Let’s try something different:

Create a C# application that asks me for 3 numbers one by one. Then return the average of those three numbers.

using OpenAI_API;
using Test; OpenAIAPI api = new OpenAIAPI("YOUR_API_KEY"); // shorthand var source = ""; await api.Completions.StreamCompletionAsync( new CompletionRequest("Create a C# application that asks me for 3 numbers one by one. Then return the average of those three numbers.", Model.DavinciText, 2000, 0.5, presencePenalty: 0.1, frequencyPenalty: 0.1), res => source += res.ToString()); //Console.WriteLine(source); var compiler = new Compiler();
var runner = new Runner(); byte[] compiled = compiler.Compile(source);
string[] none = Array.Empty<string>();
runner.Execute(compiled, none); Console.ReadLine();

Let’s see the result:

Let’s try something more difficult.

Create a C# application that asks me for a path to indicate a csv file. The csv has 2 columns, the first one: “username” and the second one “logins”. In the first column are the names of the users. In the second column is the number of logins they have done. After inputting the path to the csv file, the application should output a table with the name and the number of logins of the 5 users who have made the most logins.

For this example we have prepared a simple example table with 100 names and random numbers which we have then exported in csv:

And done!

These are just a few simple ideas, but they can be as complicated as you want them to be. As I said at the beginning, here you have access to the repository on GitHub. If you manage to create something interesting, leave it in the comments section!

Once suspended, bytehide will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, bytehide will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by bytehide will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If bytehide is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag bytehide:

Make all posts by bytehide less visible

bytehide consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging bytehide will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: bytehide

item.title: 面向初学者的 Azure：微软云平台入门指南 | Azure for Beginners: A Guide to Getting Started with Microsoft's Cloud Platform
item.description:
如果您是云计算领域的新手，您可能想知道从哪里开始。 有这么多选择，弄清楚选择哪个平台可能会让人不知所措。 但是，如果您正在寻找功能强大且用户友好的云平台，Microsoft 的 Azure 可能正是您所需要的。

在本文中，我们将带您了解 Azure 入门的基础知识。 我们将介绍 Azure 是什么、它是如何工作的，以及它的一些关键特性和优势。

首先，什么是 Azure？ 借助 Azure，您可以利用范围广泛的工具和服务，从虚拟机和数据库到机器学习和 AI。

无论你是小型初创公司还是大型企业，Azure 都可以扩展或缩减以满足你的需求。 您只需为使用的内容付费，因此您不必担心投资购买您可能不需要的昂贵硬件。

借助 Azure，你可以使用你已经熟悉的编程语言、框架和工具，因此你不必花时间学习新的。

那么如何开始使用 Azure？ 首先，您需要在 Azure 门户上创建一个帐户。 从那里，您可以探索 Azure 必须提供的各种服务和工具，并开始构建您自己的应用程序和服务。

如果您是云计算的新手，Azure 起初可能会让人望而生畏。 但凭借其友好的用户界面和强大的功能，它是初学者和经验丰富的开发人员的绝佳选择。 那么为什么不尝试一下，看看它能为您做什么。



原文:

If you're new to the world of cloud computing, you might be wondering where to start. With so many options out there, it can be overwhelming to figure out which platform to choose. But if you're looking for a powerful and user-friendly cloud platform, Microsoft's Azure might be just what you need.

In this post, we'll walk you through the basics of getting started with Azure. We'll cover what Azure is, how it works, and some of its key features and benefits.

First off, what is Azure? Azure is a cloud computing platform that allows you to build, deploy, and manage applications and services on a global scale. With Azure, you can take advantage of a wide range of tools and services, from virtual machines and databases to machine learning and AI.

One of the biggest benefits of Azure is its scalability. Whether you're a small startup or a large enterprise, Azure can scale up or down to meet your needs. You only pay for what you use, so you don't have to worry about investing in expensive hardware that you might not need.

Another key feature of Azure is its flexibility. With Azure, you can use the programming languages, frameworks, and tools that you're already familiar with, so you don't have to spend time learning new ones.

So how do you get started with Azure? First, you'll need to create an account on the Azure portal. From there, you can explore the various services and tools that Azure has to offer, and start building your own applications and services.

If you're new to cloud computing, Azure might seem intimidating at first. But with its user-friendly interface and powerful features, it's a great choice for beginners and experienced developers alike. So why not give it a try and see what it can do for you.
item.author: bhavin9920

item.title: 使用 NestJS 和 AWS 实现 Express、Heroku 技术堆栈的现代化 - PoC | Modernizing Express, Heroku tech stack with NestJS and AWS - PoC
item.description:
我们将创建一个有效的 PoC，它证明从Heroku 中托管的基于Express 的应用程序迁移到AWS 中托管的NestJS应用程序，具有基于API 网关、 Fargate和Aurora的无服务器架构并不那么复杂。 我们还将使用基于AWS CDK的IaC方法。

当我于 2022 年 9 月加入moojo担任工程主管时——beta 测试阶段即将完成，我们正准备正式推出具有发票和即时支付功能的MVP（最小可行产品） 。

后端是一个整体的Node.js应用程序，使用Express框架和Postgress数据库，两者都托管在Heroku上。 解决方案是使用函数式编程方法、 TypeScript和像Prisma这样的现代下一代 ORM（对象关系映射器），它为我们提供了类型安全和自动迁移。

这是遵循KISS（保持简单愚蠢）设计原则的简单方法，这在当时对于 MVP 解决方案来说是完美的。 这也是临时技术主管的一个有意识的决定，他启动了这个项目，并希望创建没有意见的解决方案，并将更大的决定权留给继任者。

挑战

这个设置非常好，但是当应用程序开始增长时 - 我们注意到了一些挑战。

框架

如果没有被称为依赖注入的现代设计模式，很难管理依赖关系， OpenAPI/Swagger文档通常与实际实现不同步，在没有意见的框架中自由做事对于经验不足的团队成员来说并不是最佳选择（他们需要一些指导和文档).

我们当然可以改进现有的解决方案，但另一方面 - 已经有框架提供了开箱即用的东西，所以这就像重新发明轮子。 云

在 Heroku Dashboard 中点击几下就足以设置计算或数据存储。 一些缺点是缺乏灵活性——它总是需要权衡取舍。 配置有时会受到限制，例如 Bucketeer Heroku 附加组件与 S3（简单存储服务）。

我们可能需要更先进的东西（从长远来看）。 我在其他项目中使用的AWS（亚马逊网络服务）似乎是一个自然的迁移步骤。

与每家初创公司一样，我们正忙于提供新功能，例如 IT 自由职业者的保险，因此重写所有内容不是一种选择。 我决定创建PoC（概念证明）来检查可能发生的情况并回答一些问题：

  1. 是否有可能使用 NestJS 实现新功能，但在我们的整体中保留 Express 中已经实现的功能不变（但当我们有时间时使用童子军规则逐一重写它们）？
  2. 将我们的应用程序Dockerize 化，利用 AWS 中的无服务器、 IaC（基础设施即代码）等现代方法是否容易？

嵌套

你好世界！

我开始按照NestJS 文档中的第一步创建一个新的 Nest 项目。

 npm i -g @nestjs/cli nest new moojo

这创建了工作“Hello World！” 源代码： NestJS 新项目 | 接下来我想验证的是在 NestJS 中使用现有的 Express 应用程序。 我在 Stack Overflow 问题中找到了解决方案 - Adding NestJS as express module results in Nest being restarted 。

您所要做的就是修改main.ts中的bootstrap函数并使用ExpressAdapter ：

 async function bootstrap() { const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule); const adapter = new ExpressAdapter(expressApp); const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule, adapter); await app.listen(3000); }

此时我有两个工作端点，一个用于NestJS ，一个来自Express ：

源代码： NestJS 中的 Express 应用 | 棱镜

我想为迁移到 AWS 创建一些更真实的示例，所以我决定添加一个Postgres数据库和Prisma （我们已经在 moojo 使用的 ORM）。

配方步骤中唯一缺少的是用新创建的服务更新app.module.ts

 import { Module } from '@nestjs/common'; import { AppController } from './app.controller'; import { AppService } from './app.service'; import { PostService } from './post.service'; import { PrismaService } from './prisma.service'; import { UserService } from './user.service'; @Module({ imports: [], controllers: [AppController], providers: [AppService], providers: [AppService, UserService, PostService, PrismaService], }) export class AppModule {}

源代码： Prisma 配方 | 为了轻松测试此解决方案，我决定设置OpenAPI/Swagger 文档。

您只需在main.ts中使用SwaggerModule初始化 Swagger，并在几个地方添加使用@ApiProperty装饰器。

此时 OpenAPI / Swagger UI正在运行

我能够创建用户，发布并发布它

源代码： OpenAPI/Swagger | 建筑学

如果不对我们的代码进行更大的重构就开始迁移到 AWS会很棒。 稍后我们可以转向更多的云原生解决方案，但提升和转移是伟大的第一步。

我真的很喜欢无服务器优先的方法，并在我以前的项目中大量使用它。 使用直接迁移方法 - 可能性有限，但我们仍然可以利用无服务器计算的优势，例如无需管理服务器、自动扩展、内置高可用性或按使用付费计费，这对暂存特别有利和开发环境。

所有这些都可以通过以下带有API 网关、 Fargate和Aurora Serverless 的高级架构来实现。

为了以安全可靠的方式将 API Gateway 与 Fargate 集成——我们还需要ALB（Application Load Balancer）和VPC Link 。

为了使 Fragate 任务能够从ECR（弹性容器注册表）下载 Docker 镜像，我们必须在公共子网中部署两个NAT 网关。

安全组可用于控制所有资源之间允许的流量。

您可以在 AWS 博客上找到有关此架构的更多信息，例如使用 Amazon API Gateway PrivateLink 在 AWS Fargate 上访问私有应用程序

基础架构即代码

还有许多很棒的解决方案可以帮助解决这个问题——Terraform、Pulumi、CloudFormation、 AWS CDK（云开发工具包） ——仅举几例。 对我来说——在技术堆栈的所有方面使用相同的编程语言（例如TypeScript）是一个强大的概念。 我也喜欢在给定的环境中使用原生技术，而且我已经有 AWS CDK 的经验，所以我决定使用它。

在我们创建新的 AWS CDK 应用程序之前，我们需要安装和设置一些东西。 在官方AWS CDK v2 开发人员指南中有一个很好的教程开始使用 AWS CDK 。

完成所有先决条件并安装 AWS CDK 后

我们终于可以在moojo文件夹中创建我们的第一个 AWS CDK 应用程序，稍后我们将其重命名为cloud 。

 cdk init app --language typescript

源代码： AWS CDK 应用 | 让我们开始使用具有私有和公共子网配置、可用区和NAT 网关的 VPC（虚拟私有云）构建我们的基础设施。

在这些更改之后，我们可以部署我们的应用程序

并观察我们在 CloudFormation 中创建的堆栈

您还可以验证是否在 VPC 仪表板中创建了 VPC

源代码： VPC | 接下来让我们创建Aurora Serverless （RDS 的一部分）。 我们将在我们的VPC中使用Aurora PostgreSQL引擎创建一个无服务器集群。 在我们完成 PoC 后，我们将设置诸如删除策略之类的东西以销毁以轻松清理，将配置扩展到最低限度以节省一些成本。 我们还需要启用数据 API才能在 AWS 控制台中使用查询编辑器。

如您所见 - 某些配置仅对 PoC 有意义，尚未准备好生产。 再次部署我们的堆栈后，我们可以检查我们的数据库是否正常工作。 让我们转到查询编辑器并连接到我们的数据库

您在Secrets Manager中复制Secrets manger ARN

连接后——我们可以从Prisma 生成的migration.sql文件中复制 SQL

并执行它来创建我们的表。

源代码： RDS | 下一步 - 让我们创建具有目标组、侦听器和安全组的ALB（应用程序负载均衡器） 。

源代码： ELB | 让我们使用HTTP API创建API 网关(v2)，并使用VPC Link将其与我们的 ALB（应用程序负载均衡器）集成。

源代码： GW | 现在是最后一部分的时候了——我们后端的核心。 我们将创建ECS（弹性容器服务）集群、 Fargate任务定义和服务。 我将向您展示我如何对我们的应用程序进行 Docker 化并将docker 镜像推送到ECR（弹性容器注册表） 。

您所要做的就是从 Tom Ray 的文章如何编写针对生产优化的 NestJS Dockerfile 中获取Dockerfile ，并将其保存在我们的 NestJS moojo文件夹中。

使用这个简单的代码将创建图像并将其推送到 ECR

 const image = new DockerImageAsset(this, "moojo-ecr-image", { directory: path.join(__dirname, "../../", "moojo"), platform: Platform.LINUX_AMD64, });

请记住，此ECR 配置仅适用于 PoC ，必须针对生产工作负载进行调整。 还有一个安全问题——我获取数据库 URL 并以不安全的方式将其作为环境变量传递（这必须在生产就绪的解决方案中修复，但对于 PoC 来说已经足够好了）。

部署后 - 您可以从 AWS 控制台获取 API 网关 URL

并尝试使用 Swagger UI。

如您所见，一切都在我们的本地环境中运行。

源代码： ECS | 一旦被暂停，aws-builders 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

取消暂停后，aws-builders 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 aws-builders：

降低 aws-builders 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

We will create a working PoC which proves that migration from Express based app hosted in Heroku to NestJS app hosted in AWS with serverless architecture based on API Gateway, Fargate and Aurora is not so complicated. We will also use the IaC approach based on AWS CDK.

MVP Solution

When I joined moojo as a Head of Engineering September 2022 - beta tests phase was almost done and we were preparing for the official launch of our MVP (Minimum Viable Product) with invoicing and instant payment features.

Backend was a monolithic, Node.js app using Express framework and Postgress database, both hosted on Heroku. Solution was using a functional programming approach, TypeScript and modern, next-generation ORM (Object Relational Mapper) like Prisma, which provided us with type-safety and automated migration.

It was simple approach following KISS (Keep It Simple Stupid) design principle, which was perfect at the time for the MVP solution. This was also a conscious decision the interim tech lead, who started the project and wanted to create unopinioned solution and leave bigger decisions for the successor.

Challenges

This setup was quite good, but when the application started to grow - we noticed a few challenges.

Framework

It was hard to manage dependencies without modern design patterns known as Dependency Injection, OpenAPI/Swagger documentation was often out of sync with actual implementation, freedom of doing things in unopinionated framework was not optimal for less experienced team members (they needed some guidance and documentation).

We could of course improve existing solution, but on the other hand - there are already frameworks, which provide those things out-of-the-box, so it would be like reinventing the wheel. NestJS seemed like a perfect fit for us, question was - how hard will it be to migrate.

Cloud

Heroku's advantage is that it's super easy to setup and deploy your app. A few clicks in the Heroku Dashboard is enough to setup a compute or data store. Some disadvantage is lack of flexibility - it's always about trade-offs. Heroku is built on top of AWS, but it doesn't give you access to the full richness of 200+ AWS services. Configuration is sometimes limited e.g. Bucketeer Heroku add-on vs S3 (Simple Storage Service).

We potentially needed something more advanced (in the long term). AWS (Amazon Web Services), which I used in other projects seemed like a natural migration step.

PoC (Proof of Concept)

As every startup - we are busy with delivering new features e.g. Insurance for IT freelancers, so rewriting everything was not an option. I decided to create PoC (Proof of Concept) to check what is possible and answer some questions:

  1. Is it possible to implement new features using NestJS, but leave already implemented features in Express unchanged in our monolith (but rewrite them one-by-one using The Boy Scout Rule when we have time)?
  2. Is it easy to Dockerize our app, take advantage of modern approach such as serverless, IaC (Infrastructure as Code) in AWS?

NestJS

Hello World!

I started with creating a new Nest project, by following First steps in NestJS Documentation.

npm i -g @nestjs/cli
nest new moojo

This created the working "Hello World!" NestJS app:

Source code: NestJS new project | GitHub

Express app in NestJS

Next thing I wanted to verify is using the existing Express app in NestJS. I found a solution in the Stack Overflow question - Adding NestJS as express module results in Nest being restarted.

All you have to do is to modify bootstrap function in main.ts and use ExpressAdapter:

async function bootstrap() { const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule); const adapter = new ExpressAdapter(expressApp); const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule, adapter); await app.listen(3000);
}

At this point I had two working endpoints, one for NestJS and one from Express:

Source code: Express app in NestJS | GitHub

Prisma

I wanted to create some more realistic example for the migration to AWS, so I decided to add a Postgres database and Prisma (ORM we already use at moojo).

There is a great example for that (with users and blog posts) in NestJS documentation - Prisma | NestJS, so just follow the recipe.

The only thing missing in the recipe steps was updating app.module.ts with a newly created services

import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AppController } from './app.controller';
import { AppService } from './app.service';
import { PostService } from './post.service';
import { PrismaService } from './prisma.service';
import { UserService } from './user.service'; @Module({ imports: [], controllers: [AppController], providers: [AppService], providers: [AppService, UserService, PostService, PrismaService],
})
export class AppModule {}

Source code: Prisma recipe | GitHub

OpenAPI (Swagger)

In order to easily test this solution I decided to setup OpenAPI/Swagger documentation.

Everything you need to know is again in NestJS documentation - OpenAPI (Swagger) | NestJS, so you can take a look.

You just have to initialize Swagger using SwaggerModule in main.ts and add use @ApiProperty decorators in a few places.

At this point OpenAPI / Swagger UI was working

and I was able to create user, post and publish it

Source code: OpenAPI/Swagger | GitHub

AWS

Architecture

It would be great to start migration to AWS without bigger refactoring of our code. Later we can gravitate to more cloud-native solution, but lift-and-shift is a great first step.

I really like the serverless-first approach and used it a lot in my previous projects. With lift-and-shift approach - possibilities are limited, but we still can leverage benefits of serverless computing such as no servers to manage, automatic scaling, built-in high availability or pay-for-use billing which can be especially beneficial for staging and developer environments.

All of this can be achieved with the following high level architecture with API Gateway, Fargate and Aurora Serverless.

In order to integrate API Gateway with Fargate in a secure and reliable way - we also need ALB (Application Load Balancer) and VPC Link.

Fargate and Aurora will run within a private subnet... actually two private subnets in two different Availability Zones to achieve high availability.

To enable Fragate tasks to download Docker image from ECR (Elastic Container Registry), we will have to deploy two NAT Gatways in a public subnets.

Security groups can be used to control the traffic that is allowed between all resources.

You can find out more about this architecture on AWS blog e.g. Access Private applications on AWS Fargate using Amazon API Gateway PrivateLink

Infrastructure as Code

There are many benefits of IaC (Infrastructure as Code), including speed of provisioning new environments, lower risk of human errors, improved consistency or a way to version your infrastructure.

There are also many great solutions which can help with that - Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation, AWS CDK (Cloud Development Kit) - just to name a few. For me - using the same programming language e.g. TypeScript for all aspects of the technology stack is a powerful concept. I also like to use native technologies in the given environment and I already have experience with AWS CDK, so I decided to go with it.

AWS CDK app

Before we create a new AWS CDK app, we need to install and setup a few things. There is a good tutorial Getting started with the AWS CDK in the official AWS CDK v2 Developer Guide.

When all prerequisites done and AWS CDK installed

we can finally create our First AWS CDK app in moojo folder, which we rename later to cloud.

cdk init app --language typescript

Source code: AWS CDK app | GitHub

VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)

Let's start building our infrastructure with VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) with private and public subnet configurations, Availability Zones and NAT Gateways.

vpc.cdk.ts

import { Construct } from "constructs";
import * as ec2 from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-ec2"; export class VpcCdkConstruct extends Construct { public readonly vpc: ec2.Vpc; constructor(scope: Construct, id: string) { super(scope, id); this.vpc = new ec2.Vpc(this, "moojo-vpc", { vpcName: "moojo-vpc", cidr: "10.0.0.0/16", maxAzs: 2, natGateways: 2, natGatewayProvider: ec2.NatProvider.gateway(), subnetConfiguration: [ { name: "moojo-public-subnet", subnetType: ec2.SubnetType.PUBLIC, cidrMask: 24, }, { name: "moojo-private-subnet", subnetType: ec2.SubnetType.PRIVATE_WITH_EGRESS, cidrMask: 24, }, ], }); }
}

moojo-stack.ts

import * as cdk from "aws-cdk-lib";
import { Construct } from "constructs"; import { VpcCdkConstruct } from "./vpc.cdk"; export class MoojoStack extends cdk.Stack { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) { super(scope, id, props); cdk.Tags.of(this).add("app", "Moojo"); const vpc = new VpcCdkConstruct(this, "vpc").vpc; }
}

After those changes we can deploy our app

and watch our stack being created in CloudFormation

You can also verify if VPC was created in a VPC dashboard

Source code: VPC | GitHub

RDS (Relational Database Service)

Let's create Aurora Serverless (part of RDS) next. We will create a Serverless Cluster with Aurora PostgreSQL engine in our VPC. We will setup things like removal policy to destroy to easily cleanup after we are done with PoC, scaling configuration to some bare minimum to save some costs. We also need to enable the Data API to be able to use Query Editor in AWS Console.

As you can see - some of the configuration makes sense only for PoC and it's not production ready. Point of PoC is to check and verify possible solutions, answer some questions NOT become production ready solution.

rds.cdk.ts

import { Construct } from "constructs";
import * as cdk from "aws-cdk-lib";
import * as ec2 from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-ec2";
import * as rds from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-rds"; interface RdsProps { vpc: ec2.Vpc;
} export class RdsCdkConstruct extends Construct { public readonly cluster: rds.ServerlessCluster; constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, { vpc }: RdsProps) { super(scope, id); const securityGroup = new ec2.SecurityGroup(this, "moojo-rds-sg", { vpc, securityGroupName: "moojo-rds-sg", description: "Security group for Moojo RDS (Amazon Relational Databases)", }); this.cluster = new rds.ServerlessCluster(this, "moojo-dbcluster", { clusterIdentifier: "moojo-dbcluster", defaultDatabaseName: "moojo", engine: rds.DatabaseClusterEngine.AURORA_POSTGRESQL, parameterGroup: new rds.ParameterGroup(this, "ParameterGroup", { engine: rds.DatabaseClusterEngine.auroraPostgres({ version: rds.AuroraPostgresEngineVersion.VER_10_21, // TODO: adjust supported version }), }), enableDataApi: true, // TODO: needed for Query Editor (AWS console) removalPolicy: cdk.RemovalPolicy.DESTROY, // TODO: only for PoC vpc, scaling: { // TODO: adjust it autoPause: cdk.Duration.hours(1), minCapacity: rds.AuroraCapacityUnit.ACU_2, maxCapacity: rds.AuroraCapacityUnit.ACU_2, }, securityGroups: [securityGroup], }); }
}

moojo-stack.ts

import * as cdk from "aws-cdk-lib";
import { Construct } from "constructs"; import { RdsCdkConstruct } from "./rds.cdk";
import { VpcCdkConstruct } from "./vpc.cdk"; export class MoojoStack extends cdk.Stack { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) { super(scope, id, props); cdk.Tags.of(this).add("app", "Moojo"); const vpc = new VpcCdkConstruct(this, "vpc").vpc; const rds = new RdsCdkConstruct(this, "rds", { vpc }); }
}

After deploying our stack again, we can check if our database works. Let's go to Query Editor and connect to our database

You copy Secrets manger ARN in Secrets Manager

When connected - we can copy SQL from the migration.sql file generated by Prisma

and execute it to create our tables.

Source code: RDS | GitHub

ELB (Elastic Load Balancer)

As a next step - let's create ALB (Application Load Balancer) with a target group, listener and security group.

elb.cdk.ts

import { Construct } from "constructs";
import * as ec2 from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-ec2";
import * as elbv2 from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-elasticloadbalancingv2"; interface ElbProps { vpc: ec2.Vpc;
} export class ElbCdkConstruct extends Construct { public readonly alb: elbv2.ApplicationLoadBalancer; public readonly listener: elbv2.ApplicationListener; public readonly securityGroup: ec2.SecurityGroup; public readonly targetGroup: elbv2.ApplicationTargetGroup; constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, { vpc }: ElbProps) { super(scope, id); this.securityGroup = new ec2.SecurityGroup(this, "moojo-elb-sg", { vpc, securityGroupName: "moojo-elb-sg", description: "Security group for Moojo ELB (Elastic Load Balancer)", }); this.securityGroup.addIngressRule( ec2.Peer.anyIpv4(), // TODO: restrict? ec2.Port.tcp(80), "Allow from anyone on port 80" ); this.alb = new elbv2.ApplicationLoadBalancer(this, "moojo-alb", { loadBalancerName: "moojo-alb", vpc, securityGroup: this.securityGroup, deletionProtection: false, }); this.targetGroup = new elbv2.ApplicationTargetGroup(this, "moojo-alb-tg", { vpc, targetType: elbv2.TargetType.IP, protocol: elbv2.ApplicationProtocol.HTTP, port: 80, targetGroupName: "moojo-alb-tg", }); this.listener = new elbv2.ApplicationListener(this, "moojo-alb-listener", { loadBalancer: this.alb, port: 80, protocol: elbv2.ApplicationProtocol.HTTP, defaultAction: elbv2.ListenerAction.forward([this.targetGroup]), }); }
}

moojo-stack.ts

import { RdsCdkConstruct } from "./rds.cdk";
import { VpcCdkConstruct } from "./vpc.cdk";
import { ElbCdkConstruct } from "./elb.cdk"; export class MoojoStack extends cdk.Stack { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) { super(scope, id, props); cdk.Tags.of(this).add("app", "Moojo"); const vpc = new VpcCdkConstruct(this, "vpc").vpc; const rds = new RdsCdkConstruct(this, "rds", { vpc }); const elb = new ElbCdkConstruct(this, "elb", { vpc }); }
}

Source code: ELB | GitHub

API Gateway

Let's create API Gateway (v2) with HTTP API and integrate it with our ALB (Application Load Balancer) using VPC Link.

gw.cdk.ts

import { Construct } from "constructs";
import * as apigwv2 from "@aws-cdk/aws-apigatewayv2-alpha";
import * as ec2 from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-ec2";
import * as integrations from "@aws-cdk/aws-apigatewayv2-integrations-alpha"; import { ElbCdkConstruct } from "./elb.cdk"; interface ApiGatewayProps { vpc: ec2.Vpc; elb: ElbCdkConstruct;
} export class ApiGatewayCdkConstruct extends Construct { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, { vpc, elb }: ApiGatewayProps) { super(scope, id); const httpApi = new apigwv2.HttpApi(this, "moojo-http-api", { apiName: "moojo-http-api", }); const vpcLink = new apigwv2.VpcLink(this, "moojo-vpc-link", { vpc, vpcLinkName: "moojo-vpc-link", }); const integration = new integrations.HttpAlbIntegration( "moojo-http-alb-integration", elb.listener, { method: apigwv2.HttpMethod.ANY, // TODO: restrict? vpcLink, } ); const route = new apigwv2.HttpRoute(this, "moojo-http-route", { httpApi, routeKey: apigwv2.HttpRouteKey.with("/{proxy+}", apigwv2.HttpMethod.ANY), // TODO: restrict? integration, }); }
}

moojo-stack.ts

import * as cdk from "aws-cdk-lib";
import { Construct } from "constructs"; import { ApiGatewayCdkConstruct } from "./gw.cdk";
import { ElbCdkConstruct } from "./elb.cdk";
import { RdsCdkConstruct } from "./rds.cdk";
import { VpcCdkConstruct } from "./vpc.cdk";
import { ElbCdkConstruct } from "./elb.cdk"; export class MoojoStack extends cdk.Stack { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) { super(scope, id, props); cdk.Tags.of(this).add("app", "Moojo"); const vpc = new VpcCdkConstruct(this, "vpc").vpc; const rds = new RdsCdkConstruct(this, "rds", { vpc }); const elb = new ElbCdkConstruct(this, "elb", { vpc }); const gw = new ApiGatewayCdkConstruct(this, "gw", { vpc, elb }); }
}

Source code: GW | GitHub

ECS (Elastic Container Service)

It's time for the last part - the heart of our backend. We will create ECS (Elastic Container Service) Cluster, Fargate task definition and service. I will show you how I Dockerized our app and pushed the docker image to ECR (Elastic Container Registry).

Dockerization is super simple. All you have to do is to grab Dockerfile from Tom Ray's article How to write a NestJS Dockerfile optimized for production and save it in our NestJS moojo folder.

Image will be created and pushed to ECR with this simple code

const image = new DockerImageAsset(this, "moojo-ecr-image", { directory: path.join(__dirname, "../../", "moojo"), platform: Platform.LINUX_AMD64,
});

Keep in mind that this ECR configuration is for PoC only, it has to be adjusted for the production workload. There is also a security issue - I get the database URL and pass it as an environment variable in an unsafe way (this will have to be fixed in a production ready solution, but it's good enough for the PoC).

ecs.cdk.ts

import { Construct } from "constructs";
import { DockerImageAsset, Platform } from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-ecr-assets";
import * as cdk from "aws-cdk-lib";
import * as ec2 from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-ec2";
import * as ecs from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-ecs";
import * as elbv2 from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-elasticloadbalancingv2";
import * as sm from "aws-cdk-lib/aws-secretsmanager";
import * as path from "path"; import { ElbCdkConstruct } from "./elb.cdk";
import { RdsCdkConstruct } from "./rds.cdk"; interface EcsProps { vpc: ec2.Vpc; elb: ElbCdkConstruct; rds: RdsCdkConstruct;
} export class EcsCdkConstruct extends Construct { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, { vpc, elb, rds }: EcsProps) { super(scope, id); const cluster = new ecs.Cluster(this, "moojo-ecs-cluster", { clusterName: "moojo-ecs-cluster", vpc, }); const securityGroup = new ec2.SecurityGroup(this, "moojo-ecs-sg", { vpc, securityGroupName: "moojo-ecs-sg", description: "Security group for Moojo ECS (Elastic Container Service)", }); const taskDefinition = new ecs.FargateTaskDefinition( this, "moojo-ecs-task-definition", { // TODO: adjust it memoryLimitMiB: 1024, cpu: 512, } ); const image = new DockerImageAsset(this, "moojo-ecr-image", { directory: path.join(__dirname, "../../", "moojo"), platform: Platform.LINUX_AMD64, }); const logging = new ecs.AwsLogDriver({ streamPrefix: "moojo", }); taskDefinition.addContainer("moojo-ecs-container", { containerName: "moojo-ecs-container", logging, image: ecs.ContainerImage.fromDockerImageAsset(image), portMappings: [ { containerPort: 3000, protocol: ecs.Protocol.TCP, }, ], environment: { NODE_ENV: "production", DATABASE_URL: this.getDatabaseUrlUnsafe(rds.cluster.secret!), // TODO: do it in a secure way }, }); const service = new ecs.FargateService(this, `${id}`, { serviceName: "moojo-ecs-service", taskDefinition, securityGroups: [securityGroup], cluster, // TODO: adjust it desiredCount: 1, enableExecuteCommand: true, maxHealthyPercent: 200, minHealthyPercent: 100, }); // TODO: adjust it const scaling = service.autoScaleTaskCount({ minCapacity: 1, maxCapacity: 2, }); // TODO: adjust it scaling.scaleOnCpuUtilization(`${id}-cpuscaling`, { targetUtilizationPercent: 85, scaleInCooldown: cdk.Duration.seconds(120), scaleOutCooldown: cdk.Duration.seconds(30), }); rds.cluster.connections.allowDefaultPortFrom( service, "Fargate access to Aurora" ); service.connections.allowFrom( elb.alb, ec2.Port.tcp(80), "Allow traffic from ELB" ); elb.listener.addTargets("moojo-ecs-targets", { protocol: elbv2.ApplicationProtocol.HTTP, targets: [service], healthCheck: { // TODO: adjust it healthyHttpCodes: "200", healthyThresholdCount: 3, interval: cdk.Duration.seconds(30), }, }); } // TODO: do it in a secure way!!! private getDatabaseUrlUnsafe(secret: sm.ISecret): string { const host = secret.secretValueFromJson("host").unsafeUnwrap(); const port = secret.secretValueFromJson("port").unsafeUnwrap(); const username = secret.secretValueFromJson("username").unsafeUnwrap(); const password = secret.secretValueFromJson("password").unsafeUnwrap(); const database = secret.secretValueFromJson("dbname").unsafeUnwrap(); return `postgresql://${username}:${password}@${host}:${port}/${database}`; }
}

moojo-stack.ts

import { Construct } from "constructs"; import { ApiGatewayCdkConstruct } from "./gw.cdk";
import { EcsCdkConstruct } from "./ecs.cdk";
import { ElbCdkConstruct } from "./elb.cdk";
import { RdsCdkConstruct } from "./rds.cdk";
import { VpcCdkConstruct } from "./vpc.cdk";
export class MoojoStack extends cdk.Stack { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) { super(scope, id, props); cdk.Tags.of(this).add("app", "Moojo"); const vpc = new VpcCdkConstruct(this, "vpc").vpc; const rds = new RdsCdkConstruct(this, "rds", { vpc }); const elb = new ElbCdkConstruct(this, "elb", { vpc }); const gw = new ApiGatewayCdkConstruct(this, "gw", { vpc, elb }); const ecs = new EcsCdkConstruct(this, "ecs", { vpc, elb, rds }); }
}

When deployed - you can grab API Gateway URL from the AWS Console

and give it a try using Swagger UI.

As you can see everything worked like in our local environment.

Source code: ECS | GitHub

item.title: 22 berufliches Vorwärtskommen：Ein gerader Berufsweg – Der Anfang。 | 22 berufliches Vorwärtskommen: Ein gerader Berufsweg – Der Anfang.
item.description:
穆斯塔法_A

发表于 3 月 31 日

莫恩莫恩，

祖纳赫斯特：

慕达发

夜景：
比安卡佩雷拉 - 22 年 12 月 16 日

原文:

Mustafa_A

Posted on Mar 31

Moin Moin,

gibt es eine Vorgehensweise durch die man auf einen geraden Berufsweg kommt? Ich denke ja, und diesen Gedanken, möchte ich mit dir teilen.
Zunächst:

Ein gerader Berufsweg := Ein Berufsweg der von Anfang an zu einem passt, auf dem man sein volles Potential entfalten kann und an dessen Ende man froh und dankbar ist, diesen Weg gegangen zu sein.

Um gut zu starten, setze man sich mit dem auseinander, was Robert Greene als ”primary curiosity”, in seinem Buch Mastery, bezeichnet. Dies ist ein Erlebnis, ein Phänomen oder ähnliches durch welches wir, für gewöhnlich als Kind, eine tiefe Neugierde bei uns selbst beobachtet haben.

Bei Albert Einstein soll dies eine sich bewegende Kompassnadel gewesen sein. Er fragte sich daraufhin, was es für weitere unsichtbare Kräfte, neben der Schwerkraft, gibt.
Der Rest ist Geschichte.

Was ist deine ursprüngliche Neugierde?

Beste Grüße,
Mustafa

Nachtrag:
Buchzusammenfassung – Mastery – Robert Greene↗ Dauer ungf. 9 Minuten.

„Ursprüngliche Neugierde“ in den ersten 4 Minuten.

Gideon Acromond - Dec 10 '22

Bianca Pereira - Dec 16 '22

Sidharth Mohanty - Dec 15 '22

item.title: Chrome DevTools 中缺失的部分 | The missing piece in Chrome DevTools
item.description:
它彻底改变了 Web 开发人员、QA 和后端开发人员开发、测试和调试 Web 应用程序的方式。 今天，您无法想象在没有访问 Chrome Devtools 的情况下构建 Web 应用程序。 它提供了对 Web 应用程序内部发生的事情的完整可见性，从任何给定点的 HTML DOM 到您的应用程序触发的网络请求。

如果您是 Chrome DevTools 的新手，这里有一份 Chrome DevTools 功能的初学者指南。如果您想学习高级技术，可以购买Umar Hansa提供的很棒的视频课程ModernDevTools 。

然而，重要的是要注意，虽然 Chrome DevTools 提供了在 Web 应用程序中获得可见性所需的一切，但它遗漏了很多东西，例如在 API 给出 ​​500 时测试代码，chrome 不提供任何覆盖状态的方法代码甚至 API 响应。

为什么 HTTP(s) 修改很重要？

拥有修改 HTTP(s) 请求/响应的能力，前端开发人员可以减少他们对复杂环境的依赖来测试他们的代码或对后端开发人员的 API 响应的依赖。 以下是前端开发人员可以从修改 HTTP(s) 请求中受益的一些事情。

通过加载本地运行的 JS/CSS 代码，Web 开发人员不需要在他们的机器上设置整个站点。 有时甚至不可能，尤其是当您的代码部署在客户的站点上时。

修改 HTTP 请求和响应标头的能力解锁了许多用例，例如添加身份验证标头、测试内容安全策略、调试 CORS 问题、切换功能标志等。

这是一个关于模拟人为延迟的不同方法的很好的 Reddit 线程。

还有更多用例，例如为 API 返回固定响应、在 API 未准备好时编写前端代码、发送一些调试查询参数、覆盖脚本内容以及测试不同版本的 API，但结论是 Chrome DevTools 提供的功能非常有限能力，这就是为什么开发人员必须使用低效的方法来解决这些用例。

因此，一旦您安装了 Requestly，只需转到规则界面，单击“创建新规则”按钮并为您的用例选择正确的规则类型。 最重要的是，Requestly 规则在多个浏览器和设备之间同步，您可以在共享工作区中与您的团队成员和客户协作。 为了覆盖 API 请求负载和响应主体，Requestly 覆盖了原生 XHR 和获取对象的原型，并注册了自己的拦截器。

代理模块是一个简单的基于节点的 mitm 代理，可在 — github.com/requestly/requestly-proxy获得。 一个单独的规则处理器模块应用用户定义的修改。

完整的源代码可以在 Github 上找到 — https://github.com/requestly/requestly

结论

随着 Web 社区的不断发展，Web 开发人员还需要现代工具来满足他们的需求并提高他们的工作效率。 不幸的是，Chrome DevTools 中还没有一些东西，这就是浏览器扩展存在的原因之一。 最初发布于https://sachinjain.hashnode.dev 。

一旦暂停，requestlyio 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，requestlyio 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，requestlyio 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 requestlyio 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 requestlyio：

降低 requestlyio 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 requestlyio 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Web development has come a long way since its inception, and one tool that has made a significant impact is Chrome DevTools. It has revolutionized how web developers, QAs, and backend developers develop, test & debug web apps. Today, you can’t imagine building web apps without access to Chrome Devtools. It provides complete visibility into what’s happening inside your web application, from the HTML DOM at any given point to the network requests your app fired.

Chrome DevTools also provides latency information for every API/network request, performance insights, step-by-step debugging for your source code, and an interactive console. If you are new to Chrome DevTools, here’s a beginner’s guide to Chrome DevTools features If you’re looking to learn advanced techniques, there’s a great video-based course ModernDevTools by Umar Hansa that you can buy.

However, it’s important to note that while Chrome DevTools offers everything you need to get visibility in your web app, it misses out on a lot of things like testing your code when an API gives 500, chrome doesn’t provide any way to override status code or even API responses.

Why do HTTP(s) modifications matter?

Having the ability to modify HTTP(s) requests/responses, frontend developers can reduce their dependency on the complex environment for testing their code or dependency on backend developers for API responses. Here are some of the things front-end developers can benefit from modifying HTTP(s) requests.

👉 Loading local JS/CSS bundles directly on production sites

By loading locally running JS/CSS code, web developers don’t need to set up the entire site on their machine. Sometimes it isn’t even possible especially when your code is deployed on your customers’ sites.

👉 Modifying Request & Response Headers

The ability to modify HTTP request & response headers unlocks numerous use cases like adding auth headers, testing content-security policy, debugging CORS issues, toggling feature flags, etc.

👉 Stress Testing of web apps

Stress Testing of web apps requires your code to be tested against all API scenarios like bad/invalid responses, empty responses, big API responses, API failures, API latencies, etc. Having the ability to simulate artificial delays or make APIs & third-party scripts fail can help you in testing a lot of negative scenarios. Here’s a good Reddit thread about different methods to simulate artificial delays.

There are many more use cases like returning a fixed response for an API, writing frontend code when API is not ready, sending some debug query parameters, overriding script content, and testing different version of an API but the conclusion is Chrome DevTools offers very limited capability and that’s why developers have to use inefficient methods to solve these use cases.

Introducing Requestly — Open Source HTTP(s) proxy to Intercept & Modify requests

Requestly is an open-source lightweight proxy available as a browser extension & desktop app that allows developers & QAs to Intercept & Modify network requests. Requestly is used by more than 180,000+ developers across 5000+ organizations around the globe.

⚡️ Requestly brings the power of Charles Proxy & Fiddler into the browser without worrying about proxy, SSL & VPN-related issues.

Requestly offers a complete suite of HTTP request & response modifications through different rule types e.g. URL rewrites, request payload, API response body, request & response headers , and many more using different rule types in the product.

So, once you install Requestly, just go to the rules interface, click on Create New Rule button & select the correct rule type for your use case. On top of this, Requestly rules are synced across multiple browsers & devices and you can collaborate with your teammates & customers in a shared workspace. Requestly also offers a cloud-hosted Mock Server for creating dummy mock endpoints and a file server for hosting static files like scripts & JSON files.

How Requestly works?

Requestly browser extension leverages chrome webRequest API (MV2) & chrome declarativeNetRequest API (MV3) to provide support for most of the modifications. To override API request payload & response body, Requestly overrides native XHR & fetch objects’ prototype, and registers its own interceptor.

Requestly desktop app runs a local server (aka proxy) on localhost:8171 and provides options to connect installed browsers & mobile devices to the local proxy. The proxy module is a simple node-based mitm proxy available at — github.com/requestly/requestly-proxy. A separate rule processor module applies the modifications defined by the user.

The complete source code is available on Github — https://github.com/requestly/requestly

Conclusion

With the continuously evolving web community, web developers also need modern tools to cater to their needs and make them more productive. Unfortunately, there are a few things that are not yet available in Chrome DevTools and that’s one of the reasons browser extensions exist. Requestly fills one gap in the Chrome DevTools which is modifying network requests & responses without the hassle of dealing with VPN, Proxy, or SSL issues.

Originally published at https://sachinjain.hashnode.dev.

Mrinal Walia - Dec 17 '22

item.title: 三月热门每日讨论。 主题是……科技怀旧！ | Top March Daily Discussions. And the Theme is…Nostalgia in Tech!
item.description:
发表于 3 月 31 日

好吧，这个月我们是一群怀旧的人！ 现在加入这些讨论还为时不晚：

无论我们是回顾技术的发展，想象可能发生的事情，或者只是回忆过去的美好时光，怀旧的感觉都是有价值的——也是安慰。 怀旧可以成为一种强大的动力，提醒我们我们的根源、我们的激情和我们的梦想。 当我们分享早期的故事和回忆时，它还可以帮助我们与同事和更广泛的技术社区保持联系。

但重要的是要记住，未来仍未成文，我们要以最好的方式塑造它。 在这一点上，让我们欢迎 4 月和全新的 CodeNewbie 每日讨论月。 我们希望您能参与进来并与我们的社区保持联系。 感谢您的光临。❣️

一旦暂停，codenewbieteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，codenewbieteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，codenewbieteam 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 codenewbieteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

降低 codenewbieteam 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 codenewbieteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ben Halpern for CodeNewbie

Posted on Mar 31

   

Well, we were a nostalgic bunch this month! Top CodeNewbie Daily Discussion posts in March included reminiscing about our first jobs in tech, considering what might have been with alternate career paths, and thinking back to what we wish we’d known when we were just starting out.

It’s not too late to join these discussions:

Whether we're looking back on the evolution of technology, imagining what might have been, or simply reminiscing about the good old days, there's value — and comfort — in feeling nostalgic. Nostalgia can be a powerful motivator, reminding us of our roots, our passions, and our dreams. It can also help us stay connected to our colleagues and the broader tech community as we share stories and memories of the early days.

But it's important to remember that the future is still unwritten, and that it's up to us to shape it in the best way we can. And on that note, let’s welcome April and a whole new month of CodeNewbie Daily Discussions. We hope you’ll weigh in and stay connected to our community. Thanks for being here.❣️

Matías Hernández Arellano - Feb 21

Once suspended, codenewbieteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, codenewbieteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by codenewbieteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If codenewbieteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag codenewbieteam:

Make all posts by codenewbieteam less visible

codenewbieteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging codenewbieteam will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ben

item.title: 我最喜欢的区块链开发人员 Coursera 课程 | My Favorite Coursera Courses for Blockchain Developers
item.description:
披露：这篇文章包括附属链接； 如果您从本文提供的不同链接购买产品或服务，我可能会收到补偿。

大家好，您是否对区块链技术感到好奇并正在寻找一种方法来开始您成为区块链开发人员的旅程？ 好吧，没有比参加在线课程和构建项目更好的方法了。

在课程方面， Coursera拥有广泛的区块链课程选择，从入门级课程到高级课程，涵盖智能合约和去中心化应用程序等前沿主题，由知名公司和世界一流大学提供。

作为一名区块链爱好者和开发者，我参加了其中的几门区块链课程，发现它们在我的学习之旅中非常有价值。

在本文中，我将与您分享我最喜欢的 Coursera 课程和区块链开发人员认证，它们帮助我在这个令人兴奋的领域打下了坚实的基础。

区块链对金融领域的影响最大也就不足为奇了，几乎每家大型投资银行都有自己的区块链研究部门，他们还聘请区块链专家来建立相对于竞争对手的竞争优势。

这意味着，2023 年可能是学习区块链并成为区块链开发人员或架构师的最佳时机。

如果您已经下定决心，并且您的 2023 年目标之一是学习区块链技术并成为区块链开发人员或架构师，并且正在寻找最好的在线课程，那么您来对地方了。

过去，我分享了最好的区块链认证、网站和课程，正如我所说，在本文中，我将分享最好的在线课程，从Coursera学习区块链，Coursera 是流行的在线学习平台之一，它带来了来自密歇根大学、杜克大学等世界顶尖大学以及谷歌、亚马逊、Facebook、IBM 等顶级公司的最佳学习资料。

从Udemy到Pluralsight ， 从Educative到CodeCademy ，从Udacity到FutureLearn， 从LinkedIn Learning到Edureka ，我几乎尝试了所有的在线学习平台，我发现Coursera的程序最结构化，涵盖了所有在线课程没有的相关技术掩护。

我特别喜欢Coursera 专业化，它涵盖了每个主题的细节，并提供了一个单一的资源，不仅可以学习基础知识，还可以深入掌握区块链等新技术。

如果您不知道 Coursera 专业化和专业证书是相关的、结构良好的课程的集合，它们以结构化的方式教给您特定的技能。

虽然我总是在我的列表中包含一两个 Coursera 课程，但很多人问我是否要创建一个单独的最佳 Coursera 课程列表来学习区块链，因为这是一个困难的主题之一，而且没有太多真实的学习材料可用。

因此，我们在这里列出了 Courser 提供的最佳区块链课程、认证和专业，您可以加入这些课程，在 2023 年学习这项热门技能。

我为初学者和中级开发人员提供了课程，他们希望将他们的区块链知识和技能提升到一个新的水平，最好的是你可以通过一个Coursera Plus 订阅计划加入所有这些课程，该计划不仅提供对这些课程的访问区块链课程以及许多其他 7000 多种其他技术课程，您可以使用这些课程来学习新技能并提升您的核心技能。

不要再浪费你的时间了，这是我最好的在线课程、教程、课程、培训计划和 Coursera 的专业列表，用于学习区块链。

其中许多课程还包括动手学习，有些还需要您购买书籍来完成评估和测验以深入学习区块链。

我们将首先了解 Coursera 上由知名大学提供的最佳区块链认证，然后我们将了解为想要从头学习区块链的初学者提供的区块链课程。

这是 2023 年学习区块链的最佳 Coursera 专业之一，特别是对于想要了解区块链并利用区块链网络（尤其是以太坊）开发智能合约和分布式应用程序的程序员和软件开发人员而言。

该专业有4 门课程，将教授您了解区块链所需的一切，它如何在没有任何中介的情况下促进点对点交易，以及如何在以太坊网络上开发智能合约和 dApp。

本课程由纽约州立大学和布法罗大学提供，完全在线。 如果你每周能花 4 个小时，将需要将近 4 个月的时间才能完成，并且在完成此区块链认证后你还将获得一个可共享的证书。

  • 讲师：比娜·拉玛默西
  • 提供者：纽约州立大学和布法罗大学
  • 学习区块链需要多长时间——64小时
  • 级别- 中级，需要编程知识
  • 技能 -智能合约、以太坊、区块链、Solidity、密码学、比特币、加密货币、区块链、Remix IDE、MetaMask 客户端、去中心化应用程序、Truffle IDE

谈到社会证明，这是课程中最受欢迎的区块链认证，已经有超过 70,000 名学生加入了该计划，近 5000 名学生的平均评分为 4.6，这非常了不起。

这是加入他的专业化的链接 - 区块链专业化

这是来自世界顶尖大学之一 INSEAD 的顶级区块链项目之一。 该计划旨在不仅教您什么是区块链以及它如何影响世界，而且还确定您可以在现实世界中使用区块链的机会。

此区块链认证还有 4 门课程，将教您什么是加密资产以及如何使用区块链进行交易。 您还将探索区块链的应用及其革命性的原因。

最好的是它是一门初级课程，它不仅对技术人员有用，而且对金融专业人士等想要学习和理解区块链的非技术人员也很有用。

如果您每周花费 4 小时，完成本课程大约需要 5 个月。

以下是有关本课程的主要细节和事实：

  • 讲师： Don Tapscott 和 Alex Tapscott
  • 提供者：欧洲工商管理学院
  • 学习区块链需要多长时间——80 小时
  • 级别- 初学者级别，无需编程经验
  • 领域- 计算机科学软件开发
  • 您将学习的技能- 区块链、用例、使用区块链的金融交易

谈到社会证明，超过 25,000 人信任这门课程的区块链之旅，您可以成为下一个。 该课程还拥有出色的 4.8 分（满分 5 分），这非常出色。

这是加入本课程的链接 - Blockchain Revolution

这是另一个很棒的 Coursera 课程，可以在 2023 年为初学者学习区块链技术。 本课程由 AICPA 和 CIMA 提供，本课程旨在让您概述加密资产、区块链和加密资产的安全性以及区块链技术的优势。

与前两门课程相比，这门课程时间较短，如果您每周花费 3 小时，只需一个月即可完成，但如果您每天花费 4 至 6 小时，则一周内可以轻松完成。

以下是本课程的重要细节：

  • 讲师： AICPA 和 CIMA
  • 提供方： Business Essentials 国际注册专业会计师协会
  • 学习区块链需要多长时间——12小时
  • 级别- 初学者，无需任何经验。

谈到社会证明，超过 1600 名学生已经加入了这门课程，平均评分为 4.7。 总的来说，这是一个很棒的初学者区块链认证。

这是您可以加入 Coursera 以获得区块链认证的另一个区块链专业。 该项目由 UCI 继续教育部提供，已有超过 7300 名学生加入该项目。

与此列表中的前两个认证类似，此认证也有 4 门课程，将教您区块链、密码学、安全性、拥有和组装块的基础知识和基础知识。

在此过程中，您将学习可用于区块链应用程序以及探索应用区块链概念的新机会的技能。

以下是有关此程序的重要详细信息：

  • 讲师： Stefano M. Stefan
  • 提供方： UCI 继续教育部
  • 学习区块链需要多长时间——48小时
  • 级别- 初学者

该专业的课程需要购买两本书才能完成作业：

  • 区块链基础知识：25 个步骤的非技术介绍。 货币互联网，第二卷。 总体而言，学习区块链并获得可共享证书以展示您的区块链技能的大量课程。

    金融是区块链快速建立起来的一个领域，因为它能够在没有任何中介的情况下促进交易。 自诞生以来，区块链技术彻底改变了金融服务。

    该认证还包含 4 门课程，您将在其中了解区块链在金融服务部分的作用。 作为这种专业化的结果，学生制定了战略行动计划，以确定和评估区块链技术在金融服务行业中有前途的应用。

    以下是 Coursera 中有关此区块链程序的重要详细信息：

    • 讲师： Don Tapscott 和 Alex Tapscott
    • 提供者：欧洲工商管理学院
    • 学习区块链需要多长时间——80 小时
    • 级别- 中级，需要编程知识
    • 领域 -计算机科学计算机安全和网络

    如果您是金融专业人士或金融学生，那么区块链知识可以进一步提升您的简历，这是 学习金融区块链的最佳课程之一。

    这是 Coursera 最佳区块链认证列表中的最后一个区块链认证，这个认证再次专注于供应链和金融行业。 该认证由纽约金融学院提供，专注于一套新兴的贸易融资解决方案。

    该计划还有 4 门区块链课程，您需要完成所有 4 门课程、评估和测验才能获得区块链认证。

    以下是有关此程序的重要详细信息：

    • 讲师：比娜·拉玛默西
    • 提供者：纽约金融学院
    • 学习区块链需要多长时间——64小时
    • 级别- 中级，需要编程知识

    总体而言，这是一项针对财务人员的出色区块链认证计划。 完成专业化后，您将能够评估和确定企业采购组织的供应链融资机会。

    到目前为止，我们已经看到了 Coursera 提供的最好的区块链认证和专业化课程，现在是时候看看初学者可以加入的最好的课程，从头开始学习区块链了。 这是一门实践课程，即使您对区块链一无所知也可以参加。

    这是我在网上学习区块链时遇到的最好的初级区块链在线课程之一。 如果您只是听说过比特币和以太坊，对区块链一无所知，那么您应该参加本课程。

    在向您介绍区块链之前，本课程讨论了去中心化，这是任何区块链的核心概念。

    本课程包含 4 个模块，在前三个模块中，您将了解基本的区块链概念和实现。 在第 4 个模块中，本课程讨论了下一代区块链以太坊，它展示了区块链可以通过智能合约和 dApp 为您做什么。

    在最后一个模块中，您将看到 ConsenSys 初创公司产品组合中的一个用例，它为您提供有关如何将区块链应用到现实世界的概述和灵感。 该课程是初级水平，完全适合技术和非技术观众，完成本课程大约需要 10 个小时。

    这是加入本课程的链接- 区块链：基础和用例

    这就是2023 年学习区块链技术的最佳 Coursera 课程。 本文还包括 Coursera 的顶级区块链认证，以及由 INSEAD、纽约州立大学、布法罗大学和 UBCI 等顶尖大学创建的顶级区块链认证，您可以通过这些认证从头开始学习区块链，并获得证书以添加到您的简历中。

    顺便说一下，您可以考虑参加Coursera Plus ，而不是单独参加这些课程，这是一项订阅计划，可以访问来自知名大学公司的 7000 多门课程、专业认证和 Coursera 专业。 它每月花费大约 59 美元，绝对是您升级自己的最佳投资。

    您可能喜欢的其他区块链和技术文章

    感谢您到目前为止阅读本文。 如果您喜欢这些学习区块链技术的最佳 Coursera 课程、专业化和认证，那么如果您觉得它们有用，请与您的朋友和同事分享。 如果您有任何问题或反馈，请留言。

    我的许多学生和读者都参加了这门课程，反馈非常好。



原文:

Disclosure: This post includes affiliate links; I may receive compensation if you purchase products or services from the different links provided in this article.

Hello folks, Are you curious about blockchain technology and looking for a way to get started on your journey to becoming a blockchain developer? Well, there is no better way then joining online courses and building projects.

And when it comes to courses, Coursera has a wide selection of courses on blockchain, ranging from introductory level courses to advanced ones that cover cutting-edge topics like smart contracts and decentralized applications which are offered by reputed companies and world class universities.

As a blockchain enthusiast and developer myself, I have taken several of these blockchain courses and found them to be incredibly valuable in my learning journey.

In this article, I will share with you my favorite Coursera courses and certifications for blockchain developers that have helped me build a solid foundation in this exciting field.

There is no surprise that Blockchain has the biggest impact on the Finance domain and almost every big investment bank has its own research division on Blockchain they are also hiring Blockchain experts to build a competitive advantage over their rivals.

This means, 2023 is probably the best time to learn Blockchain and become a Blockchain developer or architect.

If you already made up your mind and one of your 2023 goals is to learn Blockchain Technology and become a Blockchain developer or architect and looking for the best online courses then you have come to the right place.

In the past, I have shared the best Blockchain certifications, websites, and courses and as I said, in this article, I am going to share the best online courses to learn Blockchain from Coursera, one of the popular online learning platforms which bring the best learning material from the world's top universities like the University of Michigan, Duke University, and top companies like Google, Amazon, Facebook, IBM and more.

I have tried almost all the online learning platforms from Udemy to Pluralsight, Educative to CodeCademy, Udacity to FutureLearn, and LinkedIn Learning to Edureka, and I have found that Coursera programs are most structured,  and cover all the related technologies which many online courses don't cover.

I am particularly a big fan of Coursera specialization covers every topic in great detail and provides a single resource to not just learn basics but master a new technology like Blockchain in depth.

If you don't know Coursera specialization and Professional certificates are collections of related, well-structured courses which teach you a specific skill in a structured way.

While I always include one or two Coursera courses in my list a lot of you asked me about creating a separate list of best Coursera courses to learn Blockchain as this is one of the difficult topics and there is not much authentic study material available.

So here we are with the list of best Blockchain courses, certifications, and specializations from Courser which you can join to learn this in-demand skill in 2023.

I have included courses for both beginners and intermediate developers who want to take their Blockchain knowledge and skill to the next level and the best thing is that you can join all of these courses with a single Coursera Plus subscription plan which not only provides access to these blockchain courses but also many other 7000+ other technical courses which you can use to learn a new skill and level u your core skills.

6 Best Blockchain Certifications and Courses on Coursera in 2023

Without wasting any more of your time, here is my list of best online courses, tutorials, classes, training programs, and specializations from Coursera to learn Blockchain.

Many of these courses also included hands-on learning, some also require you to buy books to complete assessments and quizzes to learn Blockchain in depth.

We will first take a look at the best Blockchain certification available on Coursera which is offered by reported universities and then we will take a look at the Blockchain courses for beginners who want to learn Blockchain from scratch.

1. Blockchain Specialization  [Most Comprehensive Blockchain Certification]

This is one of the best Coursera specializations to learn Blockchain in 2023, particularly for programmers and software developer who wants to learn about Blockchain and develop smart contract and distributed application leveraging Blockchain network, particularly Ethereum.

This specialization has 4 courses that will teach everything you need to learn about Blockchain, how it facilitates peer-to-peer transactions without any intermediary, and then how to develop smart contracts and dApps on the Ethereum network.

This course is offered by The State University of New York & the University at Buffalo and it's completely online. It will take almost 4 months to complete if you can spend 4 hours each week and you will also get a shareable certificate after completing this Blockchain certification.

  • Instructor: Bina Ramamurthy
  • Offered by: The state university of New York, and the University at Buffalo
  • How long does it take to learn Blockchain -  64 hours
  • Level -  intermediate, programming knowledge required
  • Skills - Smart Contract, Ethereum, Blockchains, Solidity, Cryptography, Bitcoin, Cryptocurrency, Blockchain, Remix IDE, MetaMask client, Decentralized Applications, Truffle IDE

Talking about the social proof, this is the most popular Blockchain certification on courser and more than 70K students have already joined this program it has on average 4.6 rating from close to 5000 students which is remarkable.

Here is the link to join his specialization - Blockchain Specialization 

2. Blockchain Revolution [Best Blockchain Course for Non - Technical People]

This is is one of the top Blockchain programs from one of the world's top universities INSEAD. This program is designed to not only teach you what is Blockchain and how it is impacting the world but also to identify opportunities where you can use Blockchain in the real world.

This Blockchain certification also has 4 courses that will teach you what are crypto assets and how they are transacted using Blockchain. You will also explore the application of Blockchain and why it's revolutionary.

The best thing is it's a beginner-level course and it is not just useful for technical people but also for non-technical people like finance professionals who want to learn and understand Blockchain.

It will take approximately 5 months to complete this course if you can spend 4 hours each week.

Here are the key details and facts about this course:

  • Instructor: Don Tapscott and Alex Tapscott
  • Offered by: INSEAD
  • How long does it take to learn Blockchain -  80 hours
  • Level -  beginner level, no programming experience needed
  • Area -  Computer Science Software Development 
  • Skills you will learn - Blockchain, use cases, a financial transaction using Blockchain

Talking about social proof, more than 25K people have trusted this course for their Blockchain journey and you can be the next one. The course also has an excellent 4.8 rating out of 5 which is extraordinary.

Here is the link to join this course - Blockchain Revolution 

3. Introduction to Blockchain Specialization [Best Blockchain Certification for Beginners]

This is another awesome Coursera course to learn Blockchain technology in 2023 for beginners.  This course is offered by AICPA and CIMA and this course is designed for you to give an overview of Crypto assets, the security of Blockchain and Crypto assets, and the benefits of Blockchain technology.

Compared to the previous two courses this one is a shorter course and it will take just a month to complete if you spend 3 hours a week but you can easily complete this in a week if you spend 4 to 6 hours each day.

Here are important details of this course:

  • Instructor: AICPA and CIMA
  • Offered by: Business Essentials Association of International Certified Professional Accountants
  • How long does it take to learn Blockchain -  12 hours
  • Level -  Beginner, no prior experience is required. 

Talking about social proof, more than 1.6K students have already joined this course and on average it has 4.7 ratings. Overall a great Blockchain certification for Beginners.

4. Blockchain Specialization by Stefano M. Stefan UCI [Intermediate Level]

This is another Blockchain specialization you can join in Coursera to earn a Blockchain certification. This program is offered by the UCI Division of Continuing Educative and more than 7.3K students have already joined this program.

Similar to the first two certifications in this list, this one also has 4 courses that will teach you the basics and foundation of Blockchain, Cryptography, Security, having, and assembly of blocks.

Along the way, you will learn skills that can be used on blockchain applications and when exploring new opportunities for applying blockchain concepts.

Here are important details about this program:

  • Instructor: Stefano M. Stefan
  • Offered by: UCI Division of Continuing Education
  • How long does it take to learn Blockchain - 48 hours
  • Level -  Beginner

The courses in this specialization require the purchase of two books for the completion of assignments: 

  • Blockchain Basics: A Non-Technical Introduction in 25 Steps. (ISBN-13: 978-1484226032) Antonopoulos, A. M. (2017). 
  • The Internet of Money, Volume Two. (ISBN-13: 978-1947910065)

Overall a great collection of courses to learn Blockchain and also earn a sharable certificate to showcase your blockchain skills.

4. Blockchain Revolution in Financial Service [Best Blockchain Course for Finance Professionals]

Finance is one area where Blockchain is quickly establishing itself because of its ability to facilitate transactions without any intermediary. Since its inception, blockchain technology has revolutionized financial services.

This certification also contains 4 courses where you will learn about blockchains' role in the Financial service section. As a result of this specialization, students develop strategic action plans to identify and evaluate promising applications of blockchain technology in the financial services industry.

Here are important details about this Blockchain program from Coursera:

  • Instructor: Don Tapscott and Alex Tapscott
  • Offered by: INSEAD
  • How long does it take to learn Blockchain -  80 hours
  • Level -  intermediate, programming knowledge required
  • Area - Computer Science Computer Security and Networks 

If you are a finance professional or finance student then knowledge of Blockchain can further boost your CV and this is one of the best courses to learn Blockchain for Finance.

5. Supply Chain Finance and Blockchain Technology  [Best Blockchain Course for Supply Chain People]

This is the last Blockchain certification in this list of best Blockchain certifications from Coursera and this one is again focused on the Supply chain and Finance industry. This certification is offered by the New York Institute of Finance and is focused on an emerging set of solutions for trade finance. 

This program also has 4 blockchain courses underneath and you need to complete all 4 courses, assessments, and quizzes to earn the Blockchain certification.

Here are the important details about this program:

  • Instructor: Bina Ramamurthy
  • Offered by: New York Institute of Finance
  • How long does it take to learn Blockchain -  64 hours
  • Level -  intermediate, programming knowledge required

Overall a great Blockchain certification program for finance people. After completing the Specialization, you'll be able to assess and identify supply chain finance opportunities for a corporate purchasing organization.

6 Blockchain: Foundations and Use Cases [Best Blockchain Course for Beginners]

So far we have seen the best Blockchain certifications and specializations from Coursera and now is the time to look at the best course beginners can join to learn Blockchain from scratch. This is a practical course that you can join even if you don't know anything about Blockchain.

This is one of the best beginner-level Blockchain online courses I have come across to learn Blockchain online. If you just heard about Bitcoin and Ethereum and have no knowledge of Blockchain then you should join this course.

This Blockchain course on Coursera is offered by ConsenSys Academy and it is suitable for both developers and non-developers like Business Analysts, Project managers, and software architects. Before telling you about blockchain this course talks about Decentralization which is the central concept in any blockchain.

This course contains 4 modules and in the first three modules, you learn about essential blockchain concepts and implementations. In the 4th module, this course talks about Ethereum, a next-generation blockchain that shows what Blockchain can do for you with smart contracts and dApps.

In the last module, you will see a use case from the ConsenSys startup portfolio which gives you an overview and inspiration on how you can apply Blockchain to the real world. The course is beginner level and completely suitable for both technical and non-technical audiences and it will take approximately 10 hours to complete this course.

Here is the link to join this course - Blockchain: Foundations and Use Cases

That's all about the best Coursera courses to learn Blockchain Technologies in 2023. This article also includes the top Blockchain certifications from Coursera and created by top universities like INSEAD, State University of New York, University at Buffalo, and UBCI which you can take to learn Blockchain from scratch and also get a certificate to add to your resume.

By the way, Instead of joining these courses individually, you can consider taking Coursera Plus, a subscription plan which provides access to more than 7000+ courses, professional certifications, and specializations on Coursera from reputed universities companies. It cost around $59 per month and is definitely the best investment in your upgrading yourself.

Other Blockchain and Technology articles you may like

Thanks for reading this article so far. If you like these best Coursera courses, specialization, and certifications to learn Blockchain technology then please share with your friends and colleagues if you find them useful. If you have any questions or feedback, then please drop a note.

P. S. - If you are serious about learning Blockchain technologies and looking for the best Blockchain certification to start with then this Blockchain Certification on Coursera is the best resource to start with. Many of my students and readers have taken this course and feedback has been awesome.

item.title: 15 个物联网应用示例：物联网如何改变我们的生活 | 15 IoT Applications Examples: How IoT Is Changing Our Lives
item.description:
里达姆·莫迪

发表于 3 月 31 日

物联网 (IoT) 正在彻底改变我们与技术互动的方式。 物联网是指可以相互通信并共享数据的互连设备和传感器网络。 这项技术已广泛应用于各行各业，正在改变我们的生活、工作和娱乐方式。 在本文中，我们将讨论 15 个正在改变我们世界的物联网应用示例。

医疗保健中的物联网应用示例

远程患者监测：可穿戴传感器和健康追踪器等物联网设备被用于实时监测患者的健康状况。 医生可以远程跟踪患者的生命体征、服药依从性和其他与健康相关的数据，从而减少就诊次数并改善患者的治疗效果。
智能药瓶：支持物联网的药瓶可以提醒患者服药，并在错过药物时向医生发送警报。 该技术可确保患者按时服药，降低因错过服药而住院的风险。

制造业中的物联网应用示例

预测性维护：物联网传感器用于实时监控机器，预测分析用于在潜在设备故障发生之前识别它们。 该技术可以减少停机时间、提高生产率并节省成本。
质量控制：物联网传感器用于监控生产线上的产品质量，确保产品符合要求的标准。 该技术可确保识别并淘汰有缺陷的产品，从而减少浪费并提高产品质量。

交通运输中的物联网应用示例

车队管理：物联网设备用于实时监控车辆，使车队经理能够跟踪车辆的位置、监控油耗并安排维护。 该技术可以提高效率、降低成本并提高安全性。 互联汽车：支持物联网的汽车可以与其他车辆和基础设施进行通信，从而实现实时交通信息和路线优化。 这项技术可以减少拥堵、节省时间并提高安全性。

有关更多此类物联网应用示例，请阅读： www.glasierinc.com/internet-of-things-a ...

物联网在农业中的应用实例

精准农业：物联网传感器用于监测作物、土壤和天气状况，使农民能够做出有关灌溉、施肥和病虫害防治的数据驱动决策。 该技术可以提高产量、减少浪费并节省成本。
牲畜监测：传感器和 GPS 跟踪器等物联网设备用于监测牲畜的健康状况和位置，使农民能够优化饲养和繁殖实践。 这项技术可以提高生产力、减少浪费并改善动物福利。

智慧城市中的物联网应用示例

智能照明：物联网传感器用于控制路灯，降低能源消耗和维护成本。 该技术还可以通过提供更好的可见性来提高安全性。
垃圾管理：物联网传感器用于监控垃圾桶，优化垃圾收集计划并减少垃圾桶溢出。 该技术可以降低成本、提高效率并减少废物管理对环境的影响。

零售业中的物联网应用示例

库存管理：物联网传感器用于实时跟踪库存水平，使零售商能够优化库存并减少缺货情况。 该技术可以提高客户满意度、减少浪费并增加收入。
个性化营销：信标和 RFID 标签等物联网设备用于收集客户数据，使零售商能够个性化营销信息和促销活动。 该技术可以提高客户参与度、增加销售额并建立客户忠诚度。

能源领域的物联网应用示例

智能电网：物联网传感器用于监控能源使用和生产，使公用事业能够优化能源分配并减少停电。 该技术可以提高能源效率、降低成本并提高可靠性。 智能建筑：物联网传感器用于监控能源使用和占用水平，允许

降低 glasierinc3 的所有帖子的可见度
