原文:

Ridham Modi

Posted on Mar 31

The Internet of Things (IoT) is revolutionizing the way we interact with technology. IoT refers to a network of interconnected devices and sensors that can communicate with each other and share data. This technology has been widely adopted across various industries and is changing the way we live, work, and play. In this article, we will discuss 15 IoT applications examples that are transforming our world.

IoT Applications Examples in Healthcare

Remote Patient Monitoring: IoT devices such as wearable sensors and health trackers are being used to monitor patients' health in real-time. Doctors can track patients' vital signs, medication adherence, and other health-related data remotely, reducing hospital visits, and improving patient outcomes.
Smart Pill Bottles: IoT-enabled pill bottles can remind patients to take their medication and send alerts to doctors if medication is missed. This technology ensures that patients take their medication on time, reducing the risk of hospitalization due to missed medication.

IoT Applications Examples in Manufacturing

Predictive Maintenance: IoT sensors are used to monitor machines in real-time, and predictive analytics is used to identify potential equipment failures before they occur. This technology can reduce downtime, increase productivity, and save costs.
Quality Control: IoT sensors are used to monitor the quality of products on the production line, ensuring that the products meet the required standards. This technology ensures that defective products are identified and eliminated, reducing waste, and improving product quality.

IoT Applications Examples in Transportation

Fleet Management: IoT devices are used to monitor vehicles in real-time, allowing fleet managers to track the location of vehicles, monitor fuel consumption, and schedule maintenance. This technology can increase efficiency, reduce costs, and improve safety. Connected Cars: IoT-enabled cars can communicate with other vehicles and infrastructure, allowing for real-time traffic information and route optimization. This technology can reduce congestion, save time, and improve safety.

For more Such iOT Application Example Read : www.glasierinc.com/internet-of-things-a…

IoT Applications Examples in Agriculture

Precision Farming: IoT sensors are used to monitor crops, soil, and weather conditions, allowing farmers to make data-driven decisions about irrigation, fertilization, and pest control. This technology can increase yields, reduce waste, and save costs.
Livestock Monitoring: IoT devices such as sensors and GPS trackers are used to monitor the health and location of livestock, allowing farmers to optimize feeding and breeding practices. This technology can increase productivity, reduce waste, and improve animal welfare.

IoT Applications Examples in Smart Cities

Smart Lighting: IoT sensors are used to control streetlights, reducing energy consumption and maintenance costs. This technology can also improve safety by providing better visibility.
Waste Management: IoT sensors are used to monitor trash bins, optimizing waste collection schedules and reducing overflowing trash cans. This technology can reduce costs, improve efficiency, and reduce the environmental impact of waste management.

IoT Applications Examples in Retail

Inventory Management: IoT sensors are used to track inventory levels in real-time, allowing retailers to optimize stocking and reduce out-of-stock situations. This technology can improve customer satisfaction, reduce waste, and increase revenue.
Personalized Marketing: IoT devices such as beacons and RFID tags are used to collect customer data, allowing retailers to personalize marketing messages and promotions. This technology can improve customer engagement, increase sales, and build customer loyalty.

IoT Applications Examples in Energy

Smart Grids: IoT sensors are used to monitor energy usage and production, allowing utilities to optimize energy distribution and reduce blackouts. This technology can improve energy efficiency, reduce costs, and increase reliability. Smart Buildings: IoT sensors are used to monitor energy usage and occupancy levels, allowing

item.title: 选择正确的工具：JavaScript 库与框架？ | Choosing the Right tool: JavaScript Libraries vs Frameworks?
item.description:
您好，技术人员和开发人员！ 在这场伟大的战斗中，开发人员正在支持并捍卫他们喜欢的工具。 不过别担心; 我们在这里帮助您了解每一种的区别、优点和缺点，以便您可以为即将进行的项目选择理想的选项。 所以，让我们一边喝着咖啡，一边戴上我们的思维帽，探索 JavaScript 的奇异世界。

它可以应用于动画开发、表单验证、HTML/CSS 更改、用户事件管理以及与 Web API 的通信。

网页和 Web 应用程序的动态功能和交互元素由称为 JavaScript 的基本脚本语言提供支持。

尽管还有其他脚本语言，如 Python，但 JavaScript 是目前 Web 开发社区中最流行的语言。

在网页和 Web 应用程序中，动态内容是使用脚本语言 JavaScript 创建和管理的。

无需手动刷新即可在屏幕上改变的交互式表单、照片轮播和动画视觉效果都是动态内容的示例。

虽然 Python 等其他编程语言用于后端 Web 开发，但 JavaScript 主要用于前端 Web 开发。

已经开发了简化常见活动和功能的库和框架，以帮助解决 JavaScript 编程的劳动密集型方面。

这些工具旨在减少交互式表单和循环动画效果所需的重复编码量。

如果没有库或框架，程序员将不得不在每次出现常见的 JavaScript 函数时重写代码，这将是乏味且耗时的。 尽管允许开发人员构建自己的可重用函数，但库和框架为常用函数提供了预先编写的代码。 尽管库和框架都具有类似的功能，但它们的广度和规模却大相径庭。

使用 JavaScript 库可以简化 Web 应用程序的创建，JavaScript 库是提供一系列功能的预先编写的 JavaScript 代码的集合。 图书馆通常专注于为特定问题提供解决方案，例如发送 HTTP 请求、处理用户事件、处理 DOM（文档对象模型）或创建动画。 它们提供了广泛的功能，可以在不同的项目中使用，并且只需几行代码就可以添加到一个项目中。 流行的 JavaScript 库包括 jQuery、Lodash 和 Moment。 更完整的 JavaScript 工具和约定为创建 Web 应用程序提供了一种规范的方法。 一个框架通常由一系列工具和库组成，这些工具和库相互协作，为创建复杂的 Web 应用程序提供全面的解决方案。 它们提供了一种简化频繁的开发工作、实施最佳实践和组织代码的方法。 为了管理应用程序的数据、路由和可视化组件，框架通常提供统一的结构。 流行的 JavaScript 框架 Angular、React、Vue 和 Ember.js 只是几个例子。

让我们看一下表格，以便更好地比较 * JavaScript 库与 JavaScript 框架 *

关于哪个 JavaScript 库或 JavaScript 框架更好，没有一个千篇一律的答案。 库和框架都有各自的优势，适用于各种项目类型。 开发人员根据项目的特定需求选择合适的工具至关重要。

例如，在决定库或框架是否更合适时，应考虑项目的规模和范围等。 图书馆可能非常适合需要特定活动或功能的小型项目。 相反，具有广泛关注点的框架可能更适合创建复杂的 Web 应用程序。

虽然 JavaScript 库和框架都需要对语言有基本的了解，但框架通常需要更多的 JS 知识和经验，这使得库的学习曲线稍微不那么困难。 在选择开始的框架时，通常会选择 React，因为管理复杂的用户界面是前端 Web 或应用程序开发所必需的。 雇主对 React 给予了很高的评价，而且社区庞大且文档齐全。

开发人员可以使用通用语言 JavaScript。 它的库和框架对在网络开发行业工作的程序员非常有益。 框架反转程序控制，这是框架和库之间的主要区别。 它为开发人员提供必要的信息。 但是图书馆不提供细节。 相反，开发人员会在必要时调用以使用该库。 一般来说，JavaScript 提供了很好的美学和功能，并在大多数现代应用程序中使用。 由于其顶级的功能和简单的使用，它受到了全球网络开发人员的青睐。

总之，JavaScript 库和框架之间的讨论最终归结为项目的个别需求和目标。 库提供灵活性和定制选项，而框架则提供可以简化开发和维护的综合结构。 无论您选择哪种方式，紧跟 JavaScript 技术发展的步伐以确保您的项目在性能和可扩展性方面都得到了优化，这一点至关重要。

如果您正在寻找经验丰富的JavaScript 开发服务，朗讯创新是您的不二之选。 在最新的库和框架的帮助下，我们熟练的开发人员团队可以帮助您创建满足您特定需求的独特解决方案。 要详细了解我们的产品以及我们如何帮助您实现您的愿景，请立即与我们联系。



原文:

Hello, fellow techies and developers! JavaScript frameworks vs. libraries is a topic that has been the subject of several intense discussions in the web development community. Developers are taking sides and defending their preferred tools in this great battle. But do not worry; we are here to help you navigate the distinctions, benefits, and drawbacks of each so you can select the ideal option for your upcoming project. So, let's explore the strange world of JavaScript while sipping coffee and donning our thinking caps.

*What is JavaScript? *
JavaScript is a popular programming language for web development that enables developers to generate dynamic and interactive features for websites. It can be applied to the development of animations, form validation, HTML/CSS alterations, user event management, and communication with web APIs.

The dynamic functionality and interactive elements of webpages and web apps are powered by the fundamental scripting language known as JavaScript.

JavaScript is a programming language used by front-end developers to produce and manage dynamic information that users can view and interact with on their displays.

Although there are other scripting languages out there, like Python, JavaScript is by far the most popular in the web development community.

JavaScript essentially serves as the brains behind both libraries and frameworks, giving programmers the capability and interactivity, they need to build cutting-edge and interesting web experiences.

On webpages and web applications, dynamic content is created and managed using the scripting language JavaScript.

Interactive forms, photo carousels, and animated visuals that alter on screen without the need for a manual refresh are examples of dynamic content.

While other programming languages like Python are used for back-end web development, JavaScript is mostly utilized for front-end web development.

Libraries and frameworks that streamline common activities and functions have been developed to help with the labor-intensive aspects of JavaScript programming.

These tools are made to lessen the amount of repetitious coding needed for interactive forms and recurrent animation effects.

*What is Vanilla JavaScript? *
Vanilla JavaScript is the term used to describe the use of the JavaScript language without the use of libraries or pre-written code. Without libraries or frameworks, programmers would have to rewrite their code every time a common JavaScript function arose, which would be tedious and time-consuming. Although allowing developers to construct their own reusable functions, libraries, and frameworks provide pre-written code for common functions. Although libraries and frameworks both have comparable functions, their breadth and scale vary noticeably from one another.

*What are JavaScript libraries? *
The creation of web apps can be made simpler by using JavaScript libraries, which are collections of pre-written JavaScript code that offer a range of functionality. Libraries often concentrate on offering solutions for particular problems, such as sending HTTP requests, handling user events, handling the DOM (Document Object Model), or creating animations. They offer a wide range of functionality that can be utilized across different projects and can be added to a project with just a few lines of code. Popular JavaScript libraries include jQuery, Lodash, and Moment. js, and React JS to name a few.

*What exactly are JavaScript frameworks? *
The more complete tools and conventions of JavaScript offer a disciplined method for creating web apps. A framework normally consists of a collection of tools and libraries that cooperate to offer a comprehensive solution for creating complicated web applications. They provide a means of streamlining frequent development chores, enforcing best practices, and organizing code. For managing the data, routing, and visual components of the application, frameworks frequently offer a uniform structure. The popular JavaScript frameworks Angular, React, Vue, and Ember.js are just a few examples.

Let us take a look at a table for a better comparison of *JavaScript libraries vs JavaScript frameworks *

*JavaScript libraries or JavaScript frameworks which one is better? *
There is no one-size-fits-all answer as to which of JavaScript libraries or JavaScript frameworks is superior. Both libraries and frameworks have their own advantages and are appropriate for various project kinds. It's crucial for developers to select the appropriate tool based on the particular needs of their projects.

For instance, the size and scope of your project, among other things, should be taken into account when deciding whether a library or framework is more suitable. A library could be a good fit for smaller projects that call for certain activities or features. Conversely, a framework with a broad focus may be better appropriate for the creation of complex web applications.

While both JavaScript libraries and frameworks necessitate a fundamental understanding of the language, frameworks typically demand more knowledge and experience with JS, making the learning curve for libraries a little bit less difficult. When choosing a framework to start with, react is frequently chosen since managing sophisticated user interfaces is a must for front-end web or app development. Employers place a high value on React, and the community is huge and well-documented.

*How Can You Find out More About JavaScript Frameworks and Libraries? *
A developer can use the universal language JavaScript. Its libraries and frameworks are incredibly beneficial to programmers working in the web development industry. A framework inverts program control, which is the main difference between a framework and library. It provides the developer with the necessary information. But a library does not provide the detail. Instead, a developer makes a call to use the library as and when necessary. Generally, JavaScript offers great aesthetics and features and is used in the majority of modern apps. Due to its top features and simplicity of use, it is favored by web developers worldwide.

*Conclusion *
In summary, the discussion between JavaScript libraries and frameworks ultimately comes down to the individual demands and goals of a project. While libraries provide flexibility and customization options, frameworks offer a comprehensive structure that can simplify development and maintenance. Regardless of the path you take, it's critical to stay current with JavaScript technology developments to make sure your project is performance and scalability optimized.

If you're seeking experienced JavaScript development services, go no further than Lucent Innovation. With the help of the most recent libraries and frameworks, our team of skilled developers can assist you in creating unique solutions catered to your particular needs. To find out more about our offerings and how we can assist you in realizing your vision, get in touch with us right away.
item.author: miteshpatel

item.title: 成功的 MSP SLA 的关键组成部分 | The Crucial Components of a Successful MSP SLA
item.description:
众所周知，服务水平协议 (SLA) 是成功运行托管服务提供商 (MSP) 的关键部分。 良好的 SLA 应涵盖服务交付的所有领域，并考虑到您组织的特定需求，确保您获得必要的支持和服务。 在这篇博文中，我们将仅讨论有效的 MSP SLA 所必需的一些组件。 我们将探讨为什么包含对您的 MSP 提供的服务的准确定义很重要； 如何使用指标来验证结果； 指定响应时间； 了解责任和保证； 明确付款要求； 保密问题； 设置终止条件； 文档随时间变化，还有更多！ 现在让我们深入研究这些主题……

什么是 SLA 及其重要性
在商业世界中，服务水平协议或 SLA 对于确保积极的客户体验至关重要。 通过制定 SLA，企业可以设定切合实际的期望，减少误解和冲突，并改善各方之间的沟通。 重要的是要注意 SLA 不仅仅是法律文件； 它们是在服务提供商和客户之间建立信任和建立牢固关系的一种方式。 因此，拥有明确定义的 SLA 对于希望提供优质服务并保持竞争优势的企业至关重要。

定义 MSP SLA 中提供的服务范围
在 IT 外包领域，托管服务提供商 (MSP) 是提高业务效率和降低间接成本的常见选择。 但是，由于 MSP 提供的服务范围广泛，因此在选择服务水平协议 (SLA) 时确定其服务范围变得至关重要。 定义 MSP SLA 中提供的服务范围通常涉及确定 MSP 将承担的具体任务、服务持续时间以及提供的支持级别。 通过这种方式，企业可以确保清楚地了解期望的服务，并为监控 MSP 的绩效奠定基础。 因此，在选择 MSP 和协商 SLA 时，必须仔细考虑所提供服务的范围。

为服务期望制定明确的术语
在当今快节奏的世界中，客户在与企业互动时期望得到世界一流的服务。 然而，在实践中确定这意味着什么并不总是那么容易。 为服务期望开发一个清晰简洁的术语对于确保客户和员工都理解对他们的期望至关重要。 使用正式和准确的语言可以帮助消除任何混淆并确保顺畅和愉快的客户体验。 有了明确的服务期望，企业就可以建立牢固的客户关系，并为未来的发展奠定坚实的基础。

为您的 MSP 设定切合实际的可用性目标
作为 MSP，设定切合实际的可用性目标对于建立成功的业务、为客户提供他们所需的服务水平至关重要。 客户希望他们的 IT 基础架构始终可用并正常运行，因此企业为自己的团队设定可实现的目标至关重要。 通过建立切合实际的可用性目标，您的团队将清楚地了解他们的职责，使他们能够高效且有效地工作。 虽然设定目标似乎令人望而生畏，但精心设计的计划可以帮助您的团队满足客户的需求并在行业内保持良好的声誉。 请记住，可用性目标并不是企业成功的唯一衡量标准； 不断改进和适应客户不断变化的需求也很重要。

在您的 SLA 中建立安全支付系统
随着企业的扩张和接受数字交易，确保安全的支付系统对于保护金融交易至关重要。 随着电子商务迅速成为开展业务的首选方式，建立保证交易安全的支付系统仍然是许多公司最关心的问题。 安全和信任是建立客户信心的关键，因此企业必须采取措施加强在线交易的安全性。 加密技术、双因素身份验证和令牌化是企业可以用来为客户提供安全支付系统的一些措施。 通过采取必要的措施，企业可以保护消费者的个人和财务信息，同时降低可能损害其声誉的欺诈风险。

在您的 SLA 中创建争议解决流程
在任何组织中，个人之间都不可避免地会发生纠纷。 争端解决流程的创建和实施对于防止这些问题升级为更严重的问题至关重要。 通过在您的 SLA 中引入争议解决流程，涉及争议的双方都可以发表意见并达成公平公正的解决方案。 这个过程确保各方平等参与，并有充分的机会表达他们的担忧，从而得出每个人都能同意的解决方案。 通过建立正式的争议解决流程，您不仅可以快速有效地解决冲突，还可以建立信任并加强同事之间的关系。

在与 MSP 合作时，SLA 是企业的重要工具，它可以明确服务范围和服务期望。 通过清楚地了解可用服务、设定切合实际的可用性目标、建立安全支付系统以及建立争议解决流程，您可以与 MSP 建立有效且互惠互利的关系。 投资开发和维护强大的 SLA 将提供更多保证，即您正在获得尽可能最好的服务，并保护双方免受不必要的冲突，从而改善关系、提高生产力并最终使两个实体取得成功。 凭借在完善的 SLA 中明确概述的正确参数，两个组织建立了成功的合作伙伴关系，确切地知道在其整个存在过程中会发生什么。



原文:

It is no secret that Service Level Agreements (SLAs) are a crucial part of the successful running of Managed Service Providers (MSPs). An MSP SLA sets out clear expectations for both parties and helps to ensure those expectations are met. A good SLA should cover all areas of service delivery and take into account the specific needs of your organization, making sure that you receive the necessary support and services. In this blog post, we will discuss just some of the components necessary for an effective MSP SLA. We’ll look at why it's important to include an accurate definition of services provided by your MSP; how to use metrics to validate results; specify response times; understand liabilities and warranties; clarify payment requirements; confidentiality concerns; set termination conditions; document changes over time, plus much more! Let us now delve deep into these topics in detail…

What is an SLA and why it is important
In the world of business, Service Level Agreements, or SLAs are crucial to ensuring a positive customer experience. An SLA is a contract between a service provider and the customer that defines the level of service expected, including guaranteed response times, availability, and performance standards. By having an SLA in place, businesses can set realistic expectations, reduce misunderstandings and conflicts, and improve communication between parties. It is important to note that SLAs are not just legal documents; they are a way to build trust and establish a strong relationship between the service provider and the customer. Therefore, having a well-defined SLA is essential for businesses looking to provide quality service and maintain a competitive edge.

Defining the scope of services provided in an MSP SLA
In the realm of IT outsourcing, Managed Service Providers (MSPs) are a common choice to enhance business efficiency and reduce overhead costs. But, with a diverse range of services provided by MSPs, it becomes paramount to determine the scope of their services while opting for a Service Level Agreement (SLA). Defining the scope of services provided in an MSP SLA often involves determining the specific tasks the MSP will undertake, the duration of service, and the level of support provided. Through this, businesses can ensure a clear understanding of what services to expect and have a basis for monitoring the MSP's performance. As such, careful consideration of the scope of services provided is imperative when selecting an MSP and negotiating an SLA.

Developing clear terminology for service expectations
In today's fast-paced world, customers expect nothing less than world-class service when they interact with businesses. However, it's not always easy to determine what this means in practice. Developing a clear and concise terminology for service expectations is crucial in ensuring that both customers and employees understand what is expected of them. Using formal and precise language can help eliminate any confusion and ensure a smooth and enjoyable customer experience. With clear service expectations in place, businesses can build strong customer relationships and create a solid foundation for future growth.

Setting realistic availability goals for your MSP
As an MSP, setting realistic availability goals is essential to building a successful business that provides customers with the level of service they require. Customers expect their IT infrastructure to be available and functioning at all times, so it is vital that businesses set achievable goals for their own teams. By establishing realistic availability goals, your team will have a clear understanding of their responsibilities, enabling them to work efficiently and effectively. While setting goals may seem daunting, a well-crafted plan can help your team meet the needs of your customers and maintain a positive reputation within the industry. Remember, availability goals are not the only measure of success as a business; it is also important to continuously improve and adapt to the changing needs of your customers.

Establishing a secure payment system in your SLA
As businesses expand and embrace digital transactions, ensuring a secure payment system is critical in safeguarding financial transactions. With e-commerce rapidly becoming the preferred mode of conducting business, establishing a payment system that guarantees secure transactions remains a top concern for many companies. Security and trust are key in building customer confidence, and as such businesses must adopt measures that bolster the safety of online transactions. Encryption technology, two-factor authentication, and tokenization are some of the measures businesses can employ to offer their customers a secure payment system. By taking the necessary steps, businesses can protect consumers' personal and financial information while mitigating the risk of fraud that could damage their reputations.

Creating a dispute resolution process in your SLA
In any organization, disputes between individuals are bound to occur. The creation and implementation of a dispute resolution process are critical to prevent these issues from escalating into something more severe. With the introduction of a dispute resolution process in your SLA, both parties involved in the dispute can have their voices heard and come to a fair and just resolution. This process ensures that all parties are equally involved and given ample opportunity to express their concerns, leading to a solution that everyone can agree upon. By putting a formal dispute resolution process in place, not only can you resolve conflicts quickly and effectively, but you can also build trust and strengthen relationships between colleagues.

An SLA is a vital tool for businesses when engaging with an MSP, providing clarity on the scope of service and service expectations. By developing a clear understanding of available services, setting realistic availability goals, establishing secure payment systems, and establishing dispute resolution processes, you can create an effective and mutually beneficial relationship with your MSP. Investing in the development and maintenance of a robust SLA will provide added assurances that you are receiving the best possible service and also protect both parties from unnecessary conflict resulting in better relationships, productivity, and ultimately success for both entities. With the right parameters clearly outlined in a well-developed SLA, both organizations enter into a successful partnership knowing exactly what to expect throughout its existence.
item.author: dingbing1203

item.title: SPF、DKIM 和 DMARC | SPF, DKIM and DMARC
item.description:
这些都是用于保护电子邮件域免受“欺骗”和“网络钓鱼”攻击的电子邮件身份验证协议。

它是一种允许域所有者指定允许哪个服务器代表其域发送电子邮件的协议。 例如，这有助于通过验证电子邮件是否来自授权服务器来防止垃圾邮件发送者使用欺骗性地址。

在域 DNS 中配置为 TXT 记录。

它是一种向电子邮件消息添加数字签名的协议。 该协议允许接收方服务器验证消息是否由授权服务器发送，并且在传输过程中未被篡改。

发送的电子邮件使用私钥签名，公钥发布在发送服务器域的 DNS 记录中。

当收件人的电子邮件服务器收到签名的电子邮件时，它会从发件人的 DNS 记录中检索公钥并使用它来验证签名。 如果签名有效，则确认电子邮件在传输过程中未损坏，并且由授权发件人签名。

此协议必须在 DNS 记录和发送服务器上配置。

该协议建立在 SPF 和 DKIM 协议之上，允许域所有者指定电子邮件服务器应如何处理未通过 SPF 或 DKIM 检查的电子邮件。

它们是如何协同工作的？

以欺骗攻击为例，在这种攻击中，攻击者修改电子邮件或其他电子通信的标头，使其看起来像是来自实际发件人以外的来源，以便收件人认为该消息是合法的。

  • 如果服务器未经授权，电子邮件可能会被标记为垃圾邮件或完全拒绝
  • 如果签名有效，则确认电子邮件在传输过程中未被更改，并且发件人有权代表该域发送电子邮件。
  • 如果 DMARC 策略指定失败的 SPF/DKIM 验证应导致电子邮件被拒绝或标记为垃圾邮件，则收件人的电子邮件服务器将采取该操作。

如果发件人的 DNS 不包含 DNS、DKIM 和 DMARC 怎么办？

如果没有 SPF 记录，电子邮件服务器就更难验证从给定域发送的电子邮件的真实性，这可能导致垃圾邮件和网络钓鱼电子邮件被传递给收件人的风险更高。

如果没有 DKIM 记录，从域发送的电子邮件将不会使用可用于验证其真实性的数字签名进行签名，从而使攻击者更容易伪造电子邮件并进行网络钓鱼攻击。

最后，如果没有 DMARC 记录，域所有者将无法确定接收邮件服务器应如何处理未通过 SPF 和 DKIM 检查的电子邮件的策略。 这可能会导致合法电子邮件被标记为垃圾邮件或被拒绝，或者将恶意电子邮件发送给收件人。

谢谢阅读！

Photo by FLY:D on Unsplash FLY:D在Unsplash上拍摄的照片



原文:

These are all email authentication protocols that are used to protect email domains from "spoofing" and "phishing" attacks.

SPF (Sender Policy Framework)

It is a protocol that allows a domain owner to specify which server is allowed to send emails on behalf of his domain. For example, this helps prevent spammers from using spoofed addresses by verifying that the email came from an authorized server.

Is configured as TXT record in domain DNS.

DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail)

It is a protocol that adds a digital signature to an email message. This protocol allows the recipient server to verify that the message was sent by an authorised server and that it was not tampered with during transmission.

The sent email is signed with a private key and the public key is published in the DNS records of the sending server's domain.

When the recipient's email server receives the signed email, it retrieves the public key from the sender's DNS records and uses it to verify the signature. If the signature is valid, it confirms that the email was not corrupted during transmission and that it was signed by an authorized sender.

This protocol must be configured in DNS records and also on the sending server.

DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting & Conformance)

This protocol builds on the SPF and DKIM protocols and allows the domain owner to specify how e-mail servers should handle e-mails that fail the SPF or DKIM check.

DMARC allows the domain owner to receive reports on how their domain is being used for email and also to specify the action to be taken on messages that fail verification, such as marking them as spam or rejecting them altogether.

DNARC policies are published by the domain owner in the DNS records of the domain in question.

How does it all work together?

Take spoofing attacks, this is an attack where the attacker modifies the header of an email or other piece of electronic communication to make it appear to come from a source other than the actual sender, so that the recipient thinks the message is legitimate.

  • SPF: The email server first checks the sender's DNS records to see if the IP address of the sending mail server is authorized to send email on behalf of this domain. If the server is not authorized, the email will likely be marked as spam or rejected altogether
  • DKIM: If the sending mail server has passed the SPF record check and is therefore authorized to send messages, a DKIM signature check follows. If the signature is valid, it confirms that the email has not been altered during transmission and that the sender is authorized to send emails on behalf of this domain.
  • DMARC: Finally, the recipient's email server still checks the DMARC policy of the sender's domain to determine what actions to take with respect to the email it received. If the DMARC policy specifies that failed SPF/DKIM verification should result in the email being rejected or marked as SPAM, the recipient's email server will take that action.

What if the sender's DNS does not contain DNS, DKIM and DMARC?

Without an SPF record, it is more difficult for email servers to verify the authenticity of emails sent from a given domain, which can lead to a higher risk of spam and phishing emails being delivered to recipients.

Without a DKIM record, emails sent from a domain will not be signed with a digital signature that can be used to verify their authenticity, making it easier for attackers to forge emails and carry out phishing attacks.

Finally, without a DMARC record, the domain owner will not be able to determine the policy on how receiving mail servers should handle emails that fail SPF and DKIM checks. This may result in legitimate emails being marked as spam or rejected, or malicious emails being delivered to recipients.

Thank's for reading!

Photo by FLY:D on Unsplash
item.author: maymeow

item.title: 使用 OpenAI、Telegram、FastAPI 和 Deta Space 构建生成图像机器人 | Building a Generative Image Bot with OpenAI, Telegram, FastAPI and Deta Space
item.description:
您可能已经看到了一些由新的 AI 服务生成的令人惊叹的图像，例如Midjourney和 Open AI 的DALL·E 2 。

想参与其中吗？ 在本分步指南中，我们将构建一个个人 Telegram 机器人，让您可以通过手机使用 DALL·E 2。

当我们完成后，您将拥有一个功能齐全的机器人，能够根据您自己的输入创建独特的图像。 您的服务器将运行，您的图像将存储在您自己在Deta Space上的个人云中，您可以在其他项目中轻松访问和使用它们。

我们走吧。

先决条件

在开始之前，您至少需要以下内容：

选修的：

  • 此外，如果您想在本地开发，您需要在本地计算机上使用 Python

建筑学

在架构上，该机器人非常简单：

  • 界面：个人 Telegram 机器人将作为您的主要用户界面，您将在其中发送提示和接收图像。
  • 服务器+状态：在这两者之间，您将在Deta Space上运行一个FastAPI服务器以桥接 Telegram 和 DALL·E 2。该服务器会将每个图像保存在Deta Drive上的个人文件存储中。

第 1 步：创建您的机器人并获取密钥

第一步是在 Telegram 中创建机器人并从 Telegram 和 Open AI 中获取所需的密钥。

电报机器人令牌

在 Telegram 中搜索BotFather并通过单击START按钮开始与其对话。

键入/newbot ，然后按照提示设置一个新的机器人，并为您的机器人命名和用户名。 将此令牌保存在安全的地方； 稍后您将需要它。

打开 AI API 密钥

接下来，创建一个 OpenAI 帐户并获取一个OpenAI API 密钥。 此键将让您的助手使用 DALL·E 2 生成图像。

第 2 步：编写您的机器人代码

我们要编写的服务器相当简单，将做两件核心事情：

  • 使用来自 Telegram 的传入消息通过 DALL·E 2 生成图像。
  • 将生成的图像保存到您的个人云端并将它们发送回 Telegram。

完整的代码在这个存储库中，核心逻辑在main.py文件中。 所需的外部 Python 库列在requirements.txt文件中。

使用 Telegram 设置 Webhook

在 Telegram 可以向我们的服务器发送每条消息的 webhook 之前，它需要了解我们的服务器。 配置完成后，Telegram 会将事件发送到我们服务器的/open路由。

该代码假定我们已将Bot Key设置为 Deta Space ( TELEGRAM ) 中的环境变量。 它还假定它在 Deta Space 上运行，因为它从 Space 环境中获取服务器 url。 但我们会将代码部署到 Deta Space 并稍后设置环境变量。

让我们继续讨论机器人的主要逻辑。

处理来自 Telegram 的消息

机器人核心逻辑的第一步是处理来自 Telegram 的传入消息作为 DALL·E 2 API 的提示。 我们服务器中的/open路由将处理这个。

需要注意的一件事是任何人都可以向我们的机器人发送消息。 但我们不希望它们能够生成和接收图像。 因此，我们将创建一个简单的系统，通过检查电报发送的chat_id来控制谁可以访问。 稍后，我们将创建一个管理页面，让我们将chat_id添加到授权列表中。

下面是解析传入请求并根据授权chat_id列表检查chat_id的相关代码：

 from deta import Base, Drive # ... other code CONFIG = Base("config") # ... other code @app.post("/open") async def http_handler(request: Request): incoming_data = await request.json() if "message" not in incoming_data: print(incoming_data) return send_error(None, "Unknown error, lol, handling coming soon") prompt = incoming_data["message"]["text"] chat_id = incoming_data["message"]["chat"]["id"] # ... other functionality authorized_chat_ids = CONFIG.get("chat_ids") # ... other functionality if authorized_chat_ids is None or chat_id not in authorized_chat_ids.get("value"): payload = {"text": "You're not authorized!", "chat_id": chat_id} message_url = f"{BOT_URL}/sendMessage" requests.post(message_url, json=payload).json() return

请注意：我们假设我们已经在 Deta 的数据库服务Deta Base中存储了授权的chat_id列表。 在验证chat_id之后，我们可以获取消息并使用函数get_image_from_prompt将其用作 DALL·E 2 的提示。

使用 DALL-E 2 生成图像

它会得到一个图像作为响应。

 OPEN_AI_KEY = os.getenv("OPEN_AI") openai.api_key = OPEN_AI_KEY def get_image_from_prompt(prompt): response = openai.Image.create( prompt=prompt, n=1, size="512x512", response_format="b64_json", ) if "error" not in response: return { "b64img": response["data"][0]["b64_json"], "created": response["created"], } return {"error": response["error"]["message"]}

使用我们设置的默认值，我们将获得 512x512 图像响应作为 base-64 编码的 JSON。 此代码块假定我们的服务器具有之前的 Open AI 密钥，作为OPEN_AI_KEY存储在其环境中，但我们将在稍后进行设置。

现在我们已经有了一张图片，我们可以保存它并将它发送回 Telegram！

保存图像并将它们发送回 Telegram

为了保存我们的图像，我们将使用Deta Drive ，它可以让我们在我们的个人云中创建一个文件存储。 然后我们可以将图像发送回我们的 Telegram 聊天室。

两者都是在前面的/open处理函数的其余部分中使用辅助函数save_and_send_img完成的：

 from deta import Base, Drive # ... other code PHOTOS = Drive("generations") # instantiate the Deta Drive # ... other code def save_and_send_img(b64img, chat_id, prompt, timestamp): image_data = base64.b64decode(b64img) filename = f"{timestamp} - {prompt}.png" PHOTOS.put(filename, image_data) photo_payload = {"photo": image_data} message_url = f"{BOT_URL}/sendPhoto?chat_id={chat_id}&caption={prompt}" requests.post(message_url, files=photo_payload).json() return {"chat_id": chat_id, "caption": prompt} # ... other code @app.post("/open") async def http_handler(request: Request): # ... other code open_ai_resp = get_image_from_prompt(prompt) if "b64img" in open_ai_resp: return save_and_send_img( open_ai_resp["b64img"], chat_id, prompt, open_ai_resp["created"] ) if "error" in open_ai_resp: return send_error(chat_id, open_ai_resp["error"]) return send_error(chat_id, "Unknown error, lol, handling coming soon")

此函数采用 base-64 编码图像并对其进行解码。 然后它将其存储到您的云端硬盘并将其发送回您的 Telegram 机器人。

最后，我们将讨论您的机器人的管理 UI。

为您的机器人创建管理 UI

作为机器人的所有者，您会想知道机器人的状态，使用 Telegram 触发设置 webhook，并管理授权的chat_id 。

这一切都是在一个简单的 UI 中完成的，在单个index.html文件中声明，并得到JS 和 CSS的一些支持。 这个 HTML 文件由main.py中的服务器通过/ 、 /setup和/authorize路由作为Jinja模板提供。

第 3 步：运行机器人

为机器人编写代码后，您可以在 Deta Space 的个人云中部署并运行它。

在细节空间中创建项目

在这样做之前，您需要安装并登录到Deta Space CLI 。 然后，确保您的本地代码在main.py文件中包含服务器，在requirements.txt文件中包含依赖项。

获得代码后，您可以创建您的第一个太空项目。 如果您尚未从 GitHub 克隆存储库，则 CLI 可以为 Deta Space 引导您的项目。

无论哪种情况，您都会注意到有一个Spacefile ，它将告诉 Deta Space 如何运行您的应用程序。 我们要确保关于这个Spacefile的一些事情：

  • 首先，存在public_routes参数，告诉 Deta Space 将/open路由对公众开放（以便 Telegram 可以向其发送事件）。
  • 其次，在env参数下设置两个环境变量： TELEGRAM和OPEN_AI 。 我们将在我们的应用程序中使用它们分别存储 Telegram 和 Open AI 密钥。

我们的机器人的工作Spacefile示例在这里。 但为了让事情变得简单，建议克隆整个 GitHub 存储库以引导所有文件。

推送代码并设置环境变量

准备好代码并创建项目后，您可以使用 Space CLI 命令space push将本地代码推送到 Deta Space。 这将捆绑您的本地代码并将其部署为 Deta Space 上的可用应用程序。

按下后，您将在 Deta Space Canvas（仪表板）中看到一个新图标，您将在登录 Space 时看到它。 如果您单击...,然后单击Settings ，然后单击Configuration选项卡，您将能够设置环境变量。

输入之前的Open AI API 密钥和Telegram Bot 密钥，然后单击保存。

设置 Webhook 和聊天 ID

现在您已经添加了环境变量，您可以使用我们在 Telegram 中创建的机器人来配置您的助手。 从 Deta Space Canvas（仪表板）单击您的应用程序图标以访问机器人的管理面板。 如果您保存了上一步的配置变量，它应该会显示用于设置您的 webhook 的按钮。 单击设置 Webhook按钮来执行此操作。

我们必须做的最后一步是将您自己的chat_id添加到授权用户列表中：

  • 通过用户名将您的机器人添加到 Telegram 中作为联系人。
  • 然后使用命令“/chatid”与其对话，以从您的机器人获取您的聊天ID 。
  • 第三，再次访问管理界面，单击授权按钮，然后单击添加新聊天 ID按钮。
  • 最后，在输入字段中输入ID ，然后单击提交。

您现在可以生成图像了！

运行你的助手

在 Telegram 上向您的机器人发送一条消息，其中包含任何提示。 您的机器人应该使用生成的图像进行响应！

访问您的照片

您生成的所有图像都将存储在您个人的云端硬盘中。 您可以查看和下载照片，以及使用Deta Drive SDK对它们进行编程。 从您的应用程序的...菜单中单击“查看数据”选项，然后单击“驱动器”选项卡以查看您拥有的所有内容。

运行机器人的其他选项

您可以单击此处，将本教程中构建的应用程序安装到您自己的个人云中。 无需编码，您只需要：

  • 创建一个电报机器人。
  • 设置Open AI和Telegram机器人密钥。
  • 在使用机器人之前访问“/”路径来配置所有内容。

您还可以在 uvicorn 服务器运行的任何地方运行您的助手，包括在本地进行开发。 在本地情况下，您需要使用ngrok之类的工具将本地主机隧道连接到 https url（并修改代码中的DETA_SPACE_HOSTNAME变量以获取 nrgok 主机名）。

最后的话

恭喜——您已经在其中运行了一个可以通过手机访问的 AI 助手。 它将在您自己的云中运行和存储所有内容。

您可以尝试的一个想法是将图像生成功能公开为一个 API，您可以从许多其他应用程序中使用它（查看 Deta Space 上的API 密钥）。

但是享受生成各种图像的乐趣。 在我们系列的下一部分中，我们将添加与我们的助手聊天的功能，类似于 ChatGPT — 敬请期待！

特别感谢 Bighneswar Parida 在代码和单词方面的帮助。



原文:

You may have seen some of the amazing images generated by new AI services, like Midjourney and Open AI's DALL·E 2.

Want to get in on the action? In this step-by-step guide, we'll build a personal Telegram bot that will let you use DALL·E 2 from your phone.

When we're done, you'll have a fully functional bot capable of creating unique images from your own input. Your server will run and your images will be stored in your own personal cloud on Deta Space, where you can easily access and use them in other projects.

Let's get going.

Prerequisites

Before starting, out you will need the following, at a minimum:

Optional:

  • Additionally, you’ll want Python on your local machine, if you want to develop locally

Architecture

Architecturally, the bot is pretty simple:

  • Interface: A personal Telegram bot will serve as your primary user interface, where you’ll send prompts and receive images.
  • Generative Image Service: DALL·E 2 (OpenAI’s service for turning language into images) will turn the prompts into images.
  • Server + State: In between, you’ll run a FastAPI server on Deta Space to bridge Telegram and DALL·E 2. This server will save every image in a personal file store on Deta Drive.

Step 1: Creating Your Bot and Getting the Keys

The first step is to create the bot in Telegram and get the required keys from both Telegram and Open AI.

Telegram Bot Token

Search for BotFather in Telegram and start a conversation with it, by clicking the START button.

Type /newbot, and follow the prompts to set up a new bot with a name and username for your bot. BotFather will give you the bot token that you will use to authenticate your bot. Save this token somewhere safe; you will need it later.

⚠️ Keep your token secure, it can be used anyone to control your bot.

Open AI API Key

Next, create an OpenAI account and get an OpenAI API key. This key will let your assistant generate an image with DALL·E 2.

Step 2: Writing Your Bot’s Code

The server we'll write is fairly simple and will do two core things:

  • Use incoming messages from Telegram to generate images with DALL·E 2.
  • Save the generated images to your personal cloud and send them back to Telegram.

The complete code is in this repository, and the core logic lives in the main.py file. The external Python libraries required are listed in the requirements.txt file.

Setup a Webhook with Telegram

Before Telegram can send our server a webhook on every message, it needs to know about our server. The /set_webhook route, when triggered, will configure the connection with Telegram. Once configured, Telegram will send events to our server's /open route.

The code assumes we’ve set the Bot Key as an environment variable in Deta Space (TELEGRAM). It also assumes it's running on Deta Space, as it grabs the server url from the Space environment. But we’ll deploy the code to Deta Space and set the environment variables later.

Let’s move onto the main logic of our bot.

Process Messages from Telegram

The first step for the core logic of the bot is to process an incoming message from Telegram as a prompt for the DALL·E 2 API. The /open route in our server will handle this.

One thing to be aware of is that anyone can message our bot. But we don’t want them to be able to generate and receive images. So we’ll create a simple system to control who has access by checking the chat_id Telegram sends. Later on, we'll create an admin page that will let us add chat_ids to an authorized list.

Here's the relevant code that parses the incoming request and checks the chat_id against a list of authorized chat_ids:

from deta import Base, Drive
# ... other code
CONFIG = Base("config")
# ... other code

@app.post("/open")
async def http_handler(request: Request): incoming_data = await request.json() if "message" not in incoming_data: print(incoming_data) return send_error(None, "Unknown error, lol, handling coming soon") prompt = incoming_data["message"]["text"] chat_id = incoming_data["message"]["chat"]["id"] # ... other functionality
 authorized_chat_ids = CONFIG.get("chat_ids") # ... other functionality
 if authorized_chat_ids is None or chat_id not in authorized_chat_ids.get("value"): payload = {"text": "You're not authorized!", "chat_id": chat_id} message_url = f"{BOT_URL}/sendMessage" requests.post(message_url, json=payload).json() return

One note: we assume that we've stored a list of authorized chat_ids in a Deta Base, Deta's database service. After we validate the chat_id, we can grab the message and use it as a prompt for DALL·E 2 using a function get_image_from_prompt.

Generate Images with DALL-E 2

get_image_from_prompt is the function that sends the prompt to DALL·E 2, via the openai Python library. It'll get an image as a response.


OPEN_AI_KEY = os.getenv("OPEN_AI") openai.api_key = OPEN_AI_KEY def get_image_from_prompt(prompt): response = openai.Image.create( prompt=prompt, n=1, size="512x512", response_format="b64_json", ) if "error" not in response: return { "b64img": response["data"][0]["b64_json"], "created": response["created"], } return {"error": response["error"]["message"]}

Using the defaults we’ve set, we’ll get a 512x512 image response as base-64 encoded JSON. This code block assumes our server has the Open AI key from earlier, stored in its environment as OPEN_AI_KEY, but we’ll set that later.

Now that we've got an image, we can save it and send it back to Telegram!

Save the images and send them back to Telegram

To save our image, we'll use Deta Drive, which will let us create a file store in our personal cloud. We can then send it the image back to our Telegram chat.

Both are done in the rest of the /open handler function from earlier, using a helper function save_and_send_img:

from deta import Base, Drive
# ... other code

PHOTOS = Drive("generations") # instantiate the Deta Drive
# ... other code

def save_and_send_img(b64img, chat_id, prompt, timestamp): image_data = base64.b64decode(b64img) filename = f"{timestamp} - {prompt}.png" PHOTOS.put(filename, image_data) photo_payload = {"photo": image_data} message_url = f"{BOT_URL}/sendPhoto?chat_id={chat_id}&caption={prompt}" requests.post(message_url, files=photo_payload).json() return {"chat_id": chat_id, "caption": prompt} # ... other code

@app.post("/open")
async def http_handler(request: Request): # ... other code
 open_ai_resp = get_image_from_prompt(prompt) if "b64img" in open_ai_resp: return save_and_send_img( open_ai_resp["b64img"], chat_id, prompt, open_ai_resp["created"] ) if "error" in open_ai_resp: return send_error(chat_id, open_ai_resp["error"]) return send_error(chat_id, "Unknown error, lol, handling coming soon")

This function takes the base-64 encoded image and decodes it. Then it stores it to your Drive and sends it back to your Telegram bot.

Lastly, we'll talk about the management UI for your bot.

Creating a Management UI for your Bot

As the bot's owner, you'll want to know the bot's state, trigger the setup webhook with Telegram, and manage authorized chat_ids.

This is all done in a simple UI, declared in a single index.html file, with a bit of support from JS and CSS. This HTML file is served by the server in main.py via the /, /setup, and /authorize routes as a Jinja template.

Step 3: Running the Bot

Once you have written the code for your bot, you can deploy and run it out of your personal cloud in Deta Space.

Creating a Project in Deta Space

Before doing so, you’ll need to need to install and login to the Deta Space CLI. Then, ensure your local code has the server in your main.py file and dependencies in your requirements.txt file.

After you have the code, you can create your first Space Project. The CLI can bootstrap your project for Deta Space, if you haven't cloned the repository from GitHub.

In either case, you'll notice there is a Spacefile, which will tell Deta Space how to run your app. We'll want to ensure a few things about this Spacefile:

  • First, that the public_routes parameter is present, telling Deta Space to leave the /open route open to the public (so Telegram can send it events).
  • Second, to set two environment variables under the env parameter: TELEGRAM and OPEN_AI. We'll use these in our app to store the Telegram and Open AI keys, respectively.

The Spacefile also gives the app a name and sets an icon, among other things.

An example of a working Spacefile for our bot is here. But to make things easiest, it's recommended to clone the entire GitHub repository to bootstrap all the files.

Pushing Your Code and Setting Your Environment Variables

Once you’ve got your code ready and project created, you can push your local code to Deta Space with the Space CLI command space push. This will bundle your local code and deploy it as a usable app on Deta Space.

Once pushed, you’ll see a new icon in your Deta Space Canvas (the dashboard), which you'll see when you log in to Space. If you click the ..., then Settings, and then the Configuration tab, you’ll be able to set the environment variables.

Enter the Open AI API key and Telegram Bot Key from earlier and click Save.

Setting the Webhook & Chat ID

Now that you’ve added the environment variables, you can configure your assistant with the bot we’ve created in Telegram. Click your app’s icon from the Deta Space Canvas (dashboard) to visit the bot’s admin panel. (On Deta Space, you are authenticated, so this view is only accessible to you).

If you’ve saved the Configuration Variables from the last step, it should show you the button to setup your webhook. Click the Setup Webhook button to do so.

One final step we’ll have to do is add your own chat_id to the list of authorized users:

  • Add your bot in Telegram as a contact via its username.
  • Then talk to it with the command “/chatid” to get the ID of your chat from your bot.
  • Third, Visit the the admin UI again, click the authorize button and then the add new chat id button.
  • Finally, enter the ID into the input field and click submit.

You are now ready to generate images!

Running Your Assistant

Send your bot a message on Telegram, containing any prompt. Your bot should respond with the resulting image!

Accessing Your Photos

All the images you generate will be stored on your personal cloud in a Drive. You can view and download the photos, as well as program against them using the Deta Drive SDK. Click on the View Data option from the ... menu of your app, and then click the Drive tab to see everything you've got.

Other Options for Running Your Bot

You can install the app built in this tutorial in your own personal cloud in one click here. No coding is necessary, you’ll just need to:

  • create a Telegram bot.
  • set the Open AI and Telegram bot keys.
  • visit the “/” route to configure everything before using the bot.

You can also run your assistant anywhere a uvicorn server will run, including locally for development. In the local case, you’ll need to use something like ngrok to tunnel your local host to an https url (and modify the DETA_SPACE_HOSTNAME variable in the code to grab the nrgok hostname).

Final Words

Congratulations — you’ve got an AI assistant running in that can be accessed from your phone. It'll operate and store everything in your own cloud.

One idea for you to play with is to expose the image-generating function as an API, which you can use from a number of other apps (check out the API Keys on Deta Space).

But have fun generating all sorts of images. In the next part of our series, we’ll be adding the ability to chat with our assistant, similar to ChatGPT — stay tuned!

Special thanks to Bighneswar Parida for assistance with code + words.
item.author: xeust

item.title: TIL：在 Vitest 中存入嵌套的全局属性 | TIL: Stubbing nested, global properties in Vitest
item.description:
朱利安托斯卡尼

发表于 3 月 31 日

我最近升级了我们遗留代码库的一些功能以适应我们的新堆栈，其中包含：

  • 维特
  • 维泰斯特
  • 打字稿

必须重写的功能是滚动深度跟踪。 它广泛使用IntersectionObservers和一些全局属性，如innerWidth或document.visibilityState来做一些检查。 由于我很难测试document.visibilityState更改的更改，所以我写这篇文章是为了让您不必处理它们。

第一种方法：简单地设置它

不起作用，因为document.visibilityState被定义为read-only 。 但是，如果您想按照文档中所示操作window.innerWidth ，这是一个解决方案

第二种方法：使用全局存根

我的第一个想法是使用全局存根。 使用这些，您可以覆盖全局属性或对象，并提供Mock来代替使用。 我用它来按照文档中的建议对IntersectionObservers进行存根。

不幸的是，如果您在其他地方需要原始对象的功能，这将不起作用，因为它显然被提升了，这意味着它不在您当前测试的范围内。 对我来说，这没有用。 第三种方法：使用spy

您可以像这样使用它们vi.spyOn(object, "methodName") 。 它们在您检查调用function或method是否调用另一个function或method的情况下很有用。 这帮助我查明我正在测试的function是否被调用。 但是，如果使用正确的参数调用它，我现在需要。

解决方案：模拟你spy getter的返回

经过一番修改后，我发现vi.spy接受第三个参数并允许我为该setter/getter提供一个mockImplementation 。 第三个参数描述属性（而不是方法）是getter还是setter 。

因此，我最终得到了以下解决方案：

 vi.spyOn(document, 'visibilityState', 'get').mockImplementationOnce(() => 'hidden'); // and later vi.spyOn(document, 'visibilityState', 'get').mockImplementationOnce(() => 'visible');

如果您有任何问题、更好的想法或发现错误，请发表评论。

干杯!

相关代码 - 2 月 21 日

一旦暂停，jtoscani 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，jtoscani 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jtoscani 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jtoscani 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 jtoscani 的操作：

降低 jtoscani 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 jtoscani 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Julian Toscani

Posted on Mar 31

I recently upgraded some functionality of our legacy codebase to fit our new Stack containing:

  • Vue3/Nuxt3
  • Vite
  • Vitest
  • Typescript

The feature that had to be rewritten was a scroll-depth tracking. It extensively uses IntersectionObservers and some global properties like innerWidth or document.visibilityState to do some checks. As I had a hard time testing changes on document.visibilityState changes, I write this post so you won't have to deal with them.

TLDR;

vi.spyOn(document, 'visibilityState', 'get').mockImplementationOnce(() => 'hidden');

First approach: simply setting it

Does not work as document.visibilityState is defined as read-only. It is, however a solution if you want to manipulate window.innerWidth as shown in the docs

Second Approach: Using global stubs

The first idea I had was to use global stubs. With these you can overwrite a global property or object and provide Mock to use instead. I used this to stub the IntersectionObservers as suggested in the docs.

Unfortunately, if you need the functionality of the original object somewhere else this won't work as it apparently gets hoisted, meaning it is not scoped to your current test. For me, this did not work. vi.unstubAllGlobals() did not reset the stubs which may have prevented this issue as we use JSDOM.

Third Approach: Using a spy

spys are functions which observe the use of methods. You can use them like so vi.spyOn(object, "methodName"). They are usefull in cases where you check if calling a function or method calls another function or method. This helped me to find out if the function I was testing was called. However, I needed to now if it was called with the correct argument.

Solution: mocking the return of a getter you spy on

After a bit of tinkering I found out, that vi.spy accepts a third argument and allows me to provide a mockImplementation for that setter/getter. The third parameter describes if the property - instead of being a method - is a getter or setter.

Hence I ended up with the following solution:

vi.spyOn(document, 'visibilityState', 'get').mockImplementationOnce(() => 'hidden'); // and later vi.spyOn(document, 'visibilityState', 'get').mockImplementationOnce(() => 'visible');

If you have questions, better ideas or find errors, please leave a comment.

Cheers!

Code of Relevancy - Feb 21

Once suspended, jtoscani will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, jtoscani will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by jtoscani will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If jtoscani is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag jtoscani:

Make all posts by jtoscani less visible

jtoscani consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging jtoscani will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 将 OpenAI Chat Completion API 集成到 Laravel 控制台命令中 | Integrating OpenAI Chat Completion API into Laravel Console Command
item.description:
桑奇塔保罗

发表于 3 月 31 日

在这里，我们将使用 GPT-3.5-Turbo 模型集成 OpenAI Chat Completion API。 集成将通过输出结果的 Laravel 控制台命令实现。

首先创建一个名为 * ChatGPTCommand.php的命令文件
现在在应用程序名称ChatGPTSerVice中创建一个服务文件夹

<?php namespace App\Services; use GuzzleHttp\Client; use GuzzleHttp\Exception\GuzzleException; use GuzzleHttp\Psr7\Utils; use Illuminate\Console\Command; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage; class ChatGPTSerVice { /** * @throws GuzzleException */ public function chat($userInput, Command $command) { $client = new Client([ 'base_uri' => 'https://api.openai.com/v1/', ]); $headers = [ 'Content-Type' => 'application/json', 'Authorization' => 'Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY>', ]; $messages = [ ['role' => 'user', 'content' => 'Hi'], ['role' => 'assistant', 'content' => "Hi i will give you some description about lorem. if i question you then you have to answer from that description and if you do not find answer then reply only Not Found Lorem Ipsum is simply dummy text of the printing and typesetting industry. Lorem Ipsum has been the industry's standard dummy text ever since the 1500s, when an unknown printer took a galley of type and scrambled it to make a type specimen book. It has survived not only five centuries, but also the leap into electronic typesetting, remaining essentially unchanged. It was popularised in the 1960s with the release of Letraset sheets containing Lorem Ipsum passages, and more recently with desktop publishing software like Aldus PageMaker including versions of Lorem Ipsum. rules to follow, only find answer from above information. "], ['role' => 'user', 'content' => $userInput], ]; $body = json_encode([ 'model' => 'gpt-3.5-turbo', 'messages' => $messages, 'stream' => true ]); $response = $client->post('chat/completions', [ 'headers' => $headers, 'body' => $body, 'stream' => true ]); $stream = $response->getBody(); $command->info("HoodGPT"); while (!$stream->eof()) { $data = Utils::readLine($stream); $val = str_replace('data: ', '', $data); $val = str_replace(['\n', '\r'], '', $val); $json = json_decode($val, true); $str = $json ? ($json['choices'][0]['delta']['content'] ?? null) : null; if ($str) { $command->getOutput()->write($str); } } return ''; } }

该服务中的聊天功能涉及利用客户端调用 OpenAI API。 虚拟助手扮演着关键角色，其回答的准确性和相关性取决于用户问题和指示的具体性和清晰度。 此外，使用值为“true”的“stream”参数可以逐步输出响应，类似于 ChatGPT 所展示的会话风格。 这是通过使用“readLine”功能实现的，该功能可确保以经过深思熟虑和衡量的方式提供响应。

值得注意的是，OpenAI API 中还可以使用其他参数，可以在此处提供的文档中找到这些参数。 虽然在当前的实现中，请求正文中仅使用了“模型”、“消息”和“流”参数，但了解其他可用选项很重要。 这可以允许对虚拟助手的行为进行更大程度的自定义和微调，使其能够更好地满足用户的需求和期望。

现在只需在终端上运行此命令

在这里，我使用公司信息作为输出而不是 lorem ipsum。

在这种情况下，用户向虚拟助理提供具体的公司信息以及该公司参与的相应活动。然后指示助理根据此上下文回答问题。 如果问题与所提供的上下文有关，助手将以相关且具体的信息作为回应。 然而，如果问题超出了这个范围，助手将以更一般的方式回答，利用其更广泛的知识库来提供合适的回答。 这种方法允许助手提供更准确和更有针对性的信息，同时仍然能够对更一般的查询提供有用的响应。

一旦暂停，saanchitapaul 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，saanchitapaul 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，saanchitapaul 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 saanchitapaul 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 saanchitapaul 的方法：

降低 saanchitapaul 发布的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 saanchitapaul 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Sanchita Paul

Posted on Mar 31

   

Here we will be integrating the OpenAI Chat Completion API utilizing the GPT-3.5-Turbo model. The integration will be achieved through a Laravel console command that will output the results.

First create a command file named *ChatGPTCommand.php
*


<?php namespace App\Console\Commands; use App\Services\ChatGPTSerVice;
use Illuminate\Console\Command; class ChatGPTCommand extends Command
{ /** * The name and signature of the console command. * * @var string */ protected $signature = 'chat:start'; /** * The console command description. * * @var string */ protected $description = 'Command description'; /** * Execute the console command. * * @return int */ public function handle() { $this->info("Welcome to Hood ChatGPT"); $chatGptService = new ChatGPTSerVice(); $this->startChat($chatGptService); return Command::SUCCESS; } public function startChat(ChatGPTSerVice $chatGptService) { $userInput = $this->takeInput(); if (strtolower($userInput) === 'stop') { $this->info("Thank you for using Hood Chat GPT"); true; } $chatGptService->chat($userInput, $this); $this->startChat($chatGptService); } public function takeInput() { return $this->ask("\nUser:"); }
}

Now create a Services folder inside app names ChatGPTSerVice


<?php namespace App\Services;
use GuzzleHttp\Client;
use GuzzleHttp\Exception\GuzzleException;
use GuzzleHttp\Psr7\Utils;
use Illuminate\Console\Command;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage; class ChatGPTSerVice
{ /** * @throws GuzzleException */ public function chat($userInput, Command $command) { $client = new Client([ 'base_uri' => 'https://api.openai.com/v1/', ]); $headers = [ 'Content-Type' => 'application/json', 'Authorization' => 'Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY>', ]; $messages = [ ['role' => 'user', 'content' => 'Hi'], ['role' => 'assistant', 'content' => "Hi i will give you some description about lorem. if i question you then you have to answer from that description and if you do not find answer then reply only Not Found Lorem Ipsum is simply dummy text of the printing and typesetting industry. Lorem Ipsum has been the industry's standard dummy text ever since the 1500s, when an unknown printer took a galley of type and scrambled it to make a type specimen book. It has survived not only five centuries, but also the leap into electronic typesetting, remaining essentially unchanged. It was popularised in the 1960s with the release of Letraset sheets containing Lorem Ipsum passages, and more recently with desktop publishing software like Aldus PageMaker including versions of Lorem Ipsum. rules to follow,
only find answer from above information. "], ['role' => 'user', 'content' => $userInput], ]; $body = json_encode([ 'model' => 'gpt-3.5-turbo', 'messages' => $messages, 'stream' => true ]); $response = $client->post('chat/completions', [ 'headers' => $headers, 'body' => $body, 'stream' => true ]); $stream = $response->getBody(); $command->info("HoodGPT"); while (!$stream->eof()) { $data = Utils::readLine($stream); $val = str_replace('data: ', '', $data); $val = str_replace(['\n', '\r'], '', $val); $json = json_decode($val, true); $str = $json ? ($json['choices'][0]['delta']['content'] ?? null) : null; if ($str) { $command->getOutput()->write($str); } } return ''; } }

The chat function in this service involves utilizing a client to invoke the OpenAI API. The key role is played by the virtual assistant, whose accuracy and relevance of responses are contingent upon the specificity and clarity of the user's questions and instructions. Moreover, the use of the "stream" parameter with a value of "true" enables a gradual output of responses, akin to the conversational style exhibited by ChatGPT. This is accomplished by utilizing the "readLine" function, which ensures that responses are delivered in a deliberate and measured fashion.

It is worth noting that there exist additional parameters that can be utilized in the OpenAI API, which can be found in the documentation provided here . While in the current implementation, only the "model", "messages", and "stream" parameters are being utilized in the request body, it is important to be aware of the other available options. This can allow for greater customization and fine-tuning of the virtual assistant's behavior, enabling it to better meet the needs and expectations of the user.

Now just run this command on terminal

Here i am using a company info for output not lorem ipsum.

In this scenario, the user provides the virtual assistant with specific company information and the corresponding activities that the company is involved in. The assistant is then instructed to respond to a question based on this context. If the question pertains to the provided context, the assistant will respond with relevant and specific information. However, if the question falls outside of this context, the assistant will respond in a more general manner, drawing upon its broader knowledge base to provide a suitable response. This approach allows the assistant to provide more accurate and targeted information while still being able to provide helpful responses to more general inquiries.

item.title: HTML数字输入有什么问题 | What’s wrong with HTML number input
item.description:
软件开发工作是每天解决问题的过程。 开发人员喜欢从积压工作中找出复杂的问题，避免琐碎的问题。 然而，当开发人员正在调查问题并发现意外行为的原因时，任何任务都可能变成充满惊喜和侦探工作元素的冒险。

在本文中，我将分享我在创建可配置输入以键入数字方面的个人经验，一路上遇到的陷阱，最终，我将提出解决此问题的方法。

要求

让我们看看实际需要做什么。 任务是添加数字输入。 上下文是此输入是应用程序构建器的一部分，因此用户正在从一组预定义的组件创建他们的应用程序。 输入具有以下要求：

  1. 该组件应该是可配置的。 特别是它具有三个基本属性：
    • 如果 prop 从 true 切换为 false，十进制值应该被 floored。
    • 如果 prop 为 false，则最小可能值为 0。如果 prop 从 true 切换为 false，负值应更改为 0。
  2. 可以借助箭头按钮、箭头键或手动输入来更改该值。
  3. 根据所选设置，输入不应允许输入无效数字。 换句话说，允许的符号是数字 (0..9)、可选的分隔符 (,.) 和符号 (-)。
  4. 用户界面应自定义，包括箭头按钮。

规范的限制

第一个想法是使用带有类型编号的普通 HTML 输入。 它已经具有min 、 max和step属性，这些属性看起来与我们根据要求自定义行为所需的完全一样。 然而，HTML5 规范和浏览器实现有一些特点：

  1. 设置最小值限制了用户可以使用向上和向下箭头选择的值，但处理和验证手动用户输入仍然是必要的。 输入十进制数字也是如此。

  2. 小数分隔符

    • 作为分隔符，带逗号的值被认为是无效的。

    虽然英语国家/地区使用小数点作为整数和小数部分之间的首选分隔符，但许多国家/地区也使用逗号作为分隔符。 大多数标准（单位制，2019 年之前的 ISO 8601 版本，ISO 80000-1）都规定点和逗号可以同等使用。 从这个角度来看，Chrome 的行为看起来更可取，并且应该在不同的浏览器之间保持一致。

  3. 关于类型验证

    • 相反，它们将输入标记为无效并且值为空字符串。

    同样，对 Chrome 行为的支持看起来更可取，因为它可以帮助用户，不会为无效输入提供机会，并且消除了额外验证消息的必要性。

  4. 默认情况下，小数的递增/递减以与预期不同的方式工作。 它总是尝试舍入数字并忽略小数部分。 假设我们有 1.5，那么加一将得到 2，而不是 2.5。 但是，它不适用于当前版本的 Firefox。

上面的所有事情其实都是合理的，Chrome 的输入到目前为止看起来几乎是完美的。 尽管 Firefox 和 Safari 提供了更差的用户体验，但它们的使用率较低，而且这些不足对于第一个版本来说可以忽略不计。

最糟糕的是当上下箭头的自定义出现时。 为此，添加了输入组件以及自定义控件组件。

 export const NativeNumericInput = ({ value, onValueChange }) => { const { min, allowDecimals } = useConfigContext(); const [displayValue, setDisplayValue] = useState(''); const inputRef = useRef(); const max = Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER; const canIncrement = value < max; const handleIncrement = () => { inputRef.current.stepUp(); const newValue = inputRef.current.value; onChange(Number(newValue)); }; const canDecrement = value > min; const handleDecrement = () => { inputRef.current.stepDown(); const newValue = inputRef.current.value; onChange(Number(newValue)); }; const handleValueChange = (e) => { const newValue = e.target.value; setDisplayValue(newValue); if (newValue === '') { onValueChange(null); return; } onValueChange(Number(newValue)); }; return ( <div className="native-input-container"> <label htmlFor="native-input">Native Input</label> <br /> <input ref={inputRef} type="number" id="native-input" min={min} step={allowDecimals ? 'any' : 1} value={displayValue} className="input-with-custom-controls" onChange={handleValueChange} /> <NumericInputControls canIncrement={canIncrement} canDecrement={canDecrement} onIncrement={handleIncrement} onDecrement={handleDecrement} /> </div> ); }; const NumericInputControls = ({ canIncrement, canDecrement, onIncrement, onDecrement }) => { return ( <div className="numeric-input-controls"> <button onClick={onIncrement} disabled={!canIncrement}> ^ </button> <button onClick={onDecrement} disabled={!canDecrement}> ^ </button> </div> ); };

输入组件从配置中接收value作为 prop， min属性，如果allowNegative: false则为 0，并设置max以定义何时应禁用按钮。 递增和递减处理程序分别使用stepUp和stepDown方法。 它适用于整数，不适用于小数。 原因是 step 属性的值为 any， 这些方法抛出错误INVALID_STATE_ERR 。 这是因为它们的内部行为将步长值乘以 n，其中 n 是默认值为 1 的函数的参数。

这导致我们编写自己的递增和递减函数实现。 此外，这意味着按钮在包括 Firefox 在内的所有浏览器中都将以相同的方式工作。 但是 ArrowDown 和 ArrowUp 的键盘事件仍然基于数字输入的原生实现。 考虑到不再使用特定输入的好处这一事实，似乎用标准文本输入重写组件并不难，但允许为所有必要的功能实现一致的行为：类型验证、小数点分隔符、递增和小数递减。

基于文本输入的解决方案

让我们首先检查增量和减量函数是如何变化的。

 const handleIncrement = () => { const newValue = preciseMathSum(value ?? defaultValue ?? 0, 1); handleControlsValueChange(Math.min(max, newValue)); }; const handleDecrement = () => { const newValue = preciseMathSubtract(value ?? defaultValue ?? 0, 1); handleControlsValueChange(Math.max(min, newValue)); }; const handleControlsValueChange = (value) => { setDisplayValue(value.toString()); onValueChange(value); };

有方法preciseMathSum和preciseMathSubtract可以解决浮点数精度问题，因此分数部分的长度将被保留和预期。 此外，使用Math.min和Math.max有助于避免超出允许值的范围。 最小值取决于allowNegative设置，因此它是 0 或Number.MIN_SAFE_INTEGER 。 相应地，最大值允许使用不超过Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER数字进行操作。

下一步是通过关闭数字输入行为来恢复我们失去的键盘支持。

 useEffect(() => { const handleKeydown = (e) => { if (e.code === 'ArrowDown' && canDecrement) { handleDecrement(); e.preventDefault(); } else if (e.code === 'ArrowUp' && canIncrement) { handleIncrement(); e.preventDefault(); } }; inputRef.current.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeydown); return () => inputRef.current.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeydown); }, [canIncrement, canDecrement]);

它检查更改输入的可能性，并仅针对两个箭头对 keydown 事件执行相同的递增和递减操作。

最后一步是验证不允许用户将无效值放入输入中。 这些限制因组件设置而异。 用户可以键入“-”、“,”和“.” 仅当允许负数和小数时。

 const handleValueChange = (e) => { const newValue = e.target.value; if (!isValidNumberInput(newValue, { allowDecimals, allowNegative })) { return; } setDisplayValue(newValue); if (newValue === '') { onValueChange(null); return; } const newValueWithDecimalPoint = newValue.replace(',', '.'); const newNumericValue = Number(newValueWithDecimalPoint); if (newValueWithDecimalPoint !== '' && !Number.isNaN(newNumericValue)) { onValueChange(newNumericValue); } };

所有逻辑都封装在isValidNumberInput实用函数中。 它检查输入是有效数字还是可能成为有效数字的未完成输入。 它在内部使用模式匹配和检查边缘情况，同时考虑allowDecimals和allowNegative值。 用例子会更清楚。

 isValidNumberInput('-1', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: false }); // returns false isValidNumberInput('-1', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: true }); // returns true isValidNumberInput('-', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: true }); // returns true isValidNumberInput('--', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: true }); // returns false isValidNumberInput('1.5', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: false }); // returns false isValidNumberInput('1.5', { allowDecimals: true, allowNegative: false }); // returns true isValidNumberInput('1,5', { allowDecimals: true, allowNegative: false }); // returns true isValidNumberInput('1.5.', { allowDecimals: true, allowNegative: false }); // returns false

验证之后，输入将被转换为数字并在可能的情况下提交。 如果不是数字，只是设置为受控输入的显示值，则保持实际值不变。

注意：或者，可以构建一个正则表达式来检查输入的必要条件，甚至可以将其作为模式道具添加到输入中。 但是，我个人更喜欢使用更简单的正则表达式和附加条件，这些条件可能更具可读性。 此外，将此逻辑放在方法中允许我们用单元测试覆盖它。

结论

如果你想玩这个案例，有一个包含演示代码的存储库，它也与Vercel一起发布。

尽管我不得不两次执行这个任务，但它是一个很好的练习，并且很有趣地深入了解 HTML5 规范。 我希望你也喜欢这个小旅程，并找到对你有用的东西！



原文:

The software development job is a daily problem-solving process. Developers love picking up sophisticated issues from the backlog and avoiding trivial ones. However, any task can turn into an adventure with a ton of surprises and elements of a detective job when the developer is investigating a problem and discovering reasons for unexpected behavior.

In this article, I am sharing my personal experience in creating a configurable input to type numbers, facing pitfalls along the way, and eventually, I will suggest a solution for this problem.

Requirements

Let’s look at what actually had to be done. The task is to add number input. The context is that this input is a part of the application builder so users are creating their apps from a predefined set of components. The input has the following requirements:

  1. The component should be configurable. Particularly it has three essential properties:
    • defaultValue: number - should be applied when the input is empty and not touched.
    • allowDecimals: boolean - should allow typing a decimal value, increasing and decreasing by arrows should add and subtract 1 accordingly and keep the decimal part. If the prop is switched from true to false decimal value should be floored.
    • allowNegative: boolean - should allow typing negative values. If the prop is false, the minimum possible value is 0. If the prop is switched from true to false negative value should be changed to 0.
  2. The value can be changed with the help of arrow buttons, arrow keys, or manually typed.
  3. Depending on chosen settings the input should not allow typing invalid numbers. In other words, allowed symbols are digits (0..9), optionally separators (,.) and a sign (-).
  4. The UI should be customized including arrow buttons.

Restrictions by the specification

The first idea is to use a usual HTML input with a type number. It already has min, max and step properties that look exactly like what we need to customize behavior according to requirements. However, the HTML5 specification and browser implementations have a few peculiarities:

  1. Setting a minimum value restricts values that a user can choose using up and down arrows but it is still necessary to handle and validate manual user input. The same for typing decimal numbers.

  2. Decimal separator

    • Chrome works with both “,” and “.”
    • Firefox and Safari require “.” as a separator, the value with a comma is considered to be invalid.

    While English-speaking countries use a decimal point as a preferred separator between integer and fractional parts many countries use a comma as a separator as well. Most of the standards (System of Units, versions of ISO 8601 before 2019, ISO 80000-1) stipulate that both a point and a comma can be used equally. From this perspective, Chrome's behavior looks preferable and should be consistent among different browsers.

  3. On type validation

    • Chrome filters prohibited values such as letters and punctuation marks (except decimal separators).
    • Firefox and Safari allow typing any symbols. Instead, they mark the input as invalid and the value is an empty string.

    Again, the support of Chrome’s behavior looks preferable because it helps a user, doesn't provide opportunities for invalid input, and eliminates the necessity of additional validation messages.

  4. By default increment/decrement of decimals works in a different way than what was expected. It always tries to round a number and ignores a fractional part. Let’s say we have 1.5, then adding one will result in 2, not 2.5. The step can have a value equal “any” that makes increment/decrement behavior as was expected. However, it doesn’t work in the current versions of Firefox.

All the things above are actually reasonable, Chrome’s input looks almost perfect so far. Despite Firefox and Safari providing worse UX they have lower usage, and these shortages could be ignored for the first version.

The worst thing is when the customization of up and down arrows comes. For this purpose input component was added along with a custom controls component.

export const NativeNumericInput = ({ value, onValueChange }) => { const { min, allowDecimals } = useConfigContext(); const [displayValue, setDisplayValue] = useState(''); const inputRef = useRef(); const max = Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER; const canIncrement = value < max; const handleIncrement = () => { inputRef.current.stepUp(); const newValue = inputRef.current.value; onChange(Number(newValue)); }; const canDecrement = value > min; const handleDecrement = () => { inputRef.current.stepDown(); const newValue = inputRef.current.value; onChange(Number(newValue)); }; const handleValueChange = (e) => { const newValue = e.target.value; setDisplayValue(newValue); if (newValue === '') { onValueChange(null); return; } onValueChange(Number(newValue)); }; return ( <div className="native-input-container"> <label htmlFor="native-input">Native Input</label> <br /> <input ref={inputRef} type="number" id="native-input" min={min} step={allowDecimals ? 'any' : 1} value={displayValue} className="input-with-custom-controls" onChange={handleValueChange} /> <NumericInputControls canIncrement={canIncrement} canDecrement={canDecrement} onIncrement={handleIncrement} onDecrement={handleDecrement} /> </div> );
}; const NumericInputControls = ({ canIncrement, canDecrement, onIncrement, onDecrement }) => { return ( <div className="numeric-input-controls"> <button onClick={onIncrement} disabled={!canIncrement}> ^ </button> <button onClick={onDecrement} disabled={!canDecrement}> ^ </button> </div> );
};

The input component receives value as a prop, min property from the configuration which is 0 if allowNegative: false and set max to define when the buttons should be disabled. NumericInputControls is just a presentation component. The increment and decrement handlers use stepUp and stepDown methods respectively. It works fine for integers and doesn’t work for decimals. The reason is that value of the step attribute is any, and these methods throw an error INVALID_STATE_ERR. It is happening because their internal behavior multiplies the step value by n, where n is an argument of the function whose default value is 1.

This leads us to write our own implementation of increment and decrement functions. Also, it means that buttons will work the same way in all browsers including Firefox. But the keyboard events for ArrowDown and ArrowUp are still based on the native implementation of number input. Considering the fact that the benefits of specific input are not used anymore, it appears that rewriting the component with a standard text input is not much harder but allows to implement of consistent behavior for all necessary features: on type validation, decimal separators, increment and decrement for decimals.

Solution based on text input

Let’s first check how increment and decrement functions changed.

const handleIncrement = () => { const newValue = preciseMathSum(value ?? defaultValue ?? 0, 1); handleControlsValueChange(Math.min(max, newValue));
}; const handleDecrement = () => { const newValue = preciseMathSubtract(value ?? defaultValue ?? 0, 1); handleControlsValueChange(Math.max(min, newValue));
}; const handleControlsValueChange = (value) => { setDisplayValue(value.toString()); onValueChange(value);
};

There are methods preciseMathSum and preciseMathSubtract to solve a floating number precision issue so fraction part length will be kept and expected. Also, the usage of Math.min and Math.max helps to avoid exiting the range of allowed values. The minimum value depends on allowNegative setting so it is either 0 or Number.MIN_SAFE_INTEGER. The maximum allows operating with numbers no more than Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER accordingly.

The next step is to return the keyboard support that we lost by switching off the number input behavior.

useEffect(() => { const handleKeydown = (e) => { if (e.code === 'ArrowDown' && canDecrement) { handleDecrement(); e.preventDefault(); } else if (e.code === 'ArrowUp' && canIncrement) { handleIncrement(); e.preventDefault(); } }; inputRef.current.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeydown); return () => inputRef.current.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeydown);
}, [canIncrement, canDecrement]);

It checks for the possibility to change the input and does the same increment and decrement operations on the keydown event only for two arrows.

The last step is to verify that the user is not allowed to put invalid values into an input. These restrictions are different depending on the component settings. The user is able to type “-”, “,” and “.” only if negative and decimal numbers are allowed.

const handleValueChange = (e) => { const newValue = e.target.value; if (!isValidNumberInput(newValue, { allowDecimals, allowNegative })) { return; } setDisplayValue(newValue); if (newValue === '') { onValueChange(null); return; } const newValueWithDecimalPoint = newValue.replace(',', '.'); const newNumericValue = Number(newValueWithDecimalPoint); if (newValueWithDecimalPoint !== '' && !Number.isNaN(newNumericValue)) { onValueChange(newNumericValue); }
};

All the logic is encapsulated in isValidNumberInput utility function. It checks an input to be either a valid number or unfinished input which could become a valid number. Internally it uses pattern matching and checking edge cases, also considering allowDecimals and allowNegative values. It would be more clear with examples.

isValidNumberInput('-1', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: false }); // returns false
isValidNumberInput('-1', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: true }); // returns true
isValidNumberInput('-', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: true }); // returns true
isValidNumberInput('--', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: true }); // returns false
isValidNumberInput('1.5', { allowDecimals: false, allowNegative: false }); // returns false
isValidNumberInput('1.5', { allowDecimals: true, allowNegative: false }); // returns true
isValidNumberInput('1,5', { allowDecimals: true, allowNegative: false }); // returns true
isValidNumberInput('1.5.', { allowDecimals: true, allowNegative: false }); // returns false

After this verification, the input will be converted to a number and committed if possible. If it is not a number yet it is only set as a display value for controlled input, the actual value is kept the same.

Note: alternatively it is possible to build one regex to check the input for necessary conditions and even add it as a pattern prop to the input. However, I personally prefer to have simpler regex and additional conditions that could be more readable. Also, putting this logic in the method allows us to cover it with unit tests.

Conclusion

There is a repository with the demonstration code that is also published with Vercel if you want to play around with this case.

Despite the fact that I had to implement this task twice it was a nice exercise and interesting diving into the HTML5 specification. I hope you also enjoyed this small journey and found something useful for you!
item.author: titovmx
